今日(2024-07-18)Arxiv最新论文

发表于2024-07-18|更新于2024-08-09|学术会议Arxiv
|字数总计:148.5k|阅读时长:812分钟|阅读量:|评论数:

本篇博文主要展示 2024-07-18 从Arxiv.org论文网站获取的最新论文列表，自动更新，按照NLP、CV、ML、AI、IR五个大方向区分，若需要邮件定时接收，请在评论区留下你的邮箱号。

说明：每日论文数据从arxiv网站获取，每天早上11:30左右定时自动更新。

友情提示: 如何您需要邮箱接收每日论文数据，请在评论处留下你的邮箱，同样每天11:30左右邮件定时自动发送。

自然语言处理

[NLP-0] LMMs-Eval: Reality Check on the Evaluation of Large Multimodal Models
[NLP-0] LMMs-Eval：大型多峰模型评估的现实检验

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12772
作者: Kaichen Zhang,Bo Li,Peiyuan Zhang,Fanyi Pu,Joshua Adrian Cahyono,Kairui Hu,Shuai Liu,Yuanhan Zhang,Jingkang Yang,Chunyuan Li,Ziwei Liu
关键词: models necessitate wide-coverage, Large Multi-modal Models, foundation models necessitate, large foundation models, necessitate wide-coverage
中文关键词: 模型需要广泛覆盖，大型多模式模型，基础模型需要，大型基础模型需要广泛覆盖
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: Code ad leaderboard are available at this https URL and this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The advances of large foundation models necessitate wide-coverage, low-cost, and zero-contamination benchmarks. Despite continuous exploration of language model evaluations, comprehensive studies on the evaluation of Large Multi-modal Models (LMMs) remain limited. In this work, we introduce LMMS-EVAL, a unified and standardized multimodal benchmark framework with over 50 tasks and more than 10 models to promote transparent and reproducible evaluations. Although LMMS-EVAL offers comprehensive coverage, we find it still falls short in achieving low cost and zero contamination. To approach this evaluation trilemma, we further introduce LMMS-EVAL LITE, a pruned evaluation toolkit that emphasizes both coverage and efficiency. Additionally, we present Multimodal LIVEBENCH that utilizes continuously updating news and online forums to assess models’ generalization abilities in the wild, featuring a low-cost and zero-contamination evaluation approach. In summary, our work highlights the importance of considering the evaluation trilemma and provides practical solutions to navigate the trade-offs in evaluating large multi-modal models, paving the way for more effective and reliable benchmarking of LMMs. We opensource our codebase and maintain leaderboard of LIVEBENCH at this https URL and this https URL.
摘要：大型基础模型的发展需要广泛覆盖、低成本和零污染的基准。尽管对语言模型评价的研究还在继续，但对大型多通道模型(LMM)评价的全面研究仍然很有限。在这项工作中，我们引入了LMMS-EVAL，这是一个统一和标准化的多模式基准框架，具有50多项任务和10多个模型，以促进透明和可重复的评估。虽然LMMS-EVAL提供了全面的覆盖，但我们发现它在实现低成本和零污染方面仍然不足。为了解决这一评估三难问题，我们进一步介绍了LMMS-EVAL Lite，这是一个既强调覆盖率又强调效率的精简评估工具包。此外，我们还提出了多模式LIVEBENCH，它利用不断更新的新闻和在线论坛来评估模型在野外的推广能力，具有低成本和零污染的评估方法。总之，我们的工作强调了考虑评估三难境地的重要性，并提供了实用的解决方案，以在评估大型多模式模型时进行权衡，为更有效和可靠的LMM基准测试铺平了道路。我们开源我们的代码库，并在这个HTTPS URL和这个HTTPS URL上维护LIVEBENCH的排行榜。

[NLP-1] he Role of Network and Identity in the Diffusion of Hashtags
[NLP-1] 网络和身份在标签传播中的作用

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12771
作者: Aparna Ananthasubramaniam,Yufei Zhu,David Jurgens,Daniel Romero
关键词: social factors, Twitter social network, spread of behaviors, behaviors is influenced, study the effects
中文关键词: 社会因素、Twitter社交网络、行为传播、行为受到影响、研究影响
类目: ocial and Information Networks (cs.SI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computers and Society (cs.CY)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Although the spread of behaviors is influenced by many social factors, existing literature tends to study the effects of single factors – most often, properties of the social network – on the final cascade. In order to move towards a more integrated view of cascades, this paper offers the first comprehensive investigation into the role of two social factors in the diffusion of 1,337 popular hashtags representing the production of novel culture on Twitter: 1) the topology of the Twitter social network and 2) performance of each user’s probable demographic identity. Here, we show that cascades are best modeled using a combination of network and identity, rather than either factor alone. This combined model best reproduces a composite index of ten cascade properties across all 1,337 hashtags. However, there is important heterogeneity in what social factors are required to reproduce different properties of hashtag cascades. For instance, while a combined network+identity model best predicts the popularity of cascades, a network-only model has better performance in predicting cascade growth and an identity-only model in adopter composition. We are able to predict what type of hashtag is best modeled by each combination of features and use this to further improve performance. Additionally, consistent with prior literature on the combined network+identity model most outperforms the single-factor counterfactuals among hashtags used for expressing racial or regional identity, stance-taking, talking about sports, or variants of existing cultural trends with very slow- or fast-growing communicative need. In sum, our results imply the utility of multi-factor models in predicting cascades, in order to account for the varied ways in which network, identity, and other social factors play a role in the diffusion of hashtags on Twitter.
摘要：虽然行为的传播受到许多社会因素的影响，但现有的文献倾向于研究单一因素–最常见的是社会网络的属性–对最终级联的影响。为了更全面地了解层叠效应，本文首次对代表Twitter上新文化生产的1337个流行标签的传播过程中两个社会因素的作用进行了全面的调查：1)Twitter社交网络的拓扑结构；2)每个用户可能的人口统计身份的表现。在这里，我们展示了级联最适合使用网络和身份的组合来建模，而不是单独使用这两个因素中的一个。这个组合模型最好地再现了所有1,337个标签的10个级联属性的综合索引。然而，在需要哪些社会因素来重现标签级联的不同属性方面，存在着重要的异质性。例如，虽然组合的网络+身份模型最能预测级联的受欢迎程度，但在预测级联增长方面，纯网络模型具有更好的性能，而在采用者构成中，仅身份模型具有更好的性能。我们能够预测每种功能组合最适合哪种类型的hashtag，并利用这一点进一步提高性能。此外，与之前关于网络+身份组合模式的文献一致，Most优于用于表达种族或地区身份、立场、谈论体育或具有非常缓慢或快速增长的交流需求的现有文化趋势的变体的标签中的单因素反事实。总而言之，我们的结果暗示了多因素模型在预测级联中的实用性，以解释网络、身份和其他社会因素在Twitter上标签传播中发挥作用的不同方式。

[NLP-2] HDLCopilot: Hardware Design Library Querying with Natural Language
[NLP-2] HDLCopilot：使用自然语言查询硬件设计库

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12749
作者: Manar Abdelatty,Sherief Reda
关键词: Process Design Kits, standard cell libraries, Design Kits, engineers routinely work, fabrication labs
中文关键词: 过程设计套件、标准单元库、设计套件、工程师常规工作、制造实验室
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 7 pages, 8 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Hardware design engineers routinely work with multiple Process Design Kits (PDKs) from various fabrication labs, each containing several standard cell libraries, optimized for specific metric such as speed, power, or density. These libraries include multiple views such as liberty files for timing information, LEF files for abstract layout details, and technology LEF for process design rules. Navigating this complex landscape to retrieve specific information about gates or design rules is often time-consuming and error-prone. To address this, we present HDLCopilot, an LLM-powered PDK query system that allows engineers to streamline interactions with PDKs in natural language format, making information retrieval accurate and more efficient. HDLCopilot achieves an accuracy of 94.23% on an evaluation set comprised of diverse and complex natural language queries. HDLCopilot positions itself as a powerful assistant in the hardware design process, enhancing productivity and reducing potential human errors.
摘要：硬件设计工程师经常使用来自不同制造实验室的多个工艺设计工具包(PDK)，每个都包含几个标准单元库，针对速度、功率或密度等特定指标进行了优化。这些库包括多个视图，例如用于计时信息的自由文件、用于抽象布局细节的LEF文件和用于工艺设计规则的技术LEF。在这种复杂的环境中导航以检索有关大门或设计规则的特定信息通常既耗时又容易出错。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了HDLCopilot，这是一个基于LLM的PDK查询系统，允许工程师以自然语言格式简化与PDK的交互，使信息检索更加准确和高效。HDLCopilot在包含各种复杂自然语言查询的评估集上达到了94.23%的准确率。HDLCopilot将自己定位为硬件设计过程中的强大助手，提高了生产率并减少了潜在的人为错误。

[NLP-3] A LLM Benchmark based on the Minecraft Builder Dialog Agent Task
[NLP-3] 基于Minecraft Builder对话代理任务的LLM基准

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12734
作者: Chris Madge,Massimo Poesio
关键词: adapting the Minecraft, Minecraft builder task, spatially orientated tasks, LLM benchmark suitable, Minecraft builder
中文关键词: 调整Minecraft、Minecraft构建器任务、空间导向任务、LLM基准适合、Minecraft构建器
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this work we proposing adapting the Minecraft builder task into an LLM benchmark suitable for evaluating LLM ability in spatially orientated tasks, and informing builder agent design. Previous works have proposed corpora with varying complex structures, and human written instructions. We instead attempt to provide a comprehensive synthetic benchmark for testing builder agents over a series of distinct tasks that comprise of common building operations. We believe this approach allows us to probe specific strengths and weaknesses of different agents, and test the ability of LLMs in the challenging area of spatial reasoning and vector based math.
摘要：在这项工作中，我们提议将Minecraft构建器任务调整为LLM基准，该基准适合评估空间导向任务中的LLM能力，并为构建器代理设计提供信息。之前的作品提出了具有不同复杂结构和人类书面指令的文集。相反，我们试图提供全面的合成基准，用于在一系列由常见建筑操作组成的不同任务中测试建筑商代理。我们相信这种方法使我们能够探索不同代理的具体优点和缺点，并测试LLM在空间推理和基于载体的数学这一具有挑战性的领域的能力。

[NLP-4] Is Sarcasm Detection A Step-by-Step Reasoning Process in Large Language Models?
[NLP-4] 讽刺检测是大型语言模型中的分步推理过程吗？

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12725
作者: Ben Yao,Yazhou Zhang,Qiuchi Li,Jing Qin
关键词: large language models, solve complex problems, steps significantly improves, reasoning steps significantly, intermediate reasoning steps
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，解决复杂问题，步骤显着改进，推理步骤显着，中间推理步骤
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 13 pages, 2 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Elaborating a series of intermediate reasoning steps significantly improves the ability of large language models (LLMs) to solve complex problems, as such steps would evoke LLMs to think sequentially. However, human sarcasm understanding is often considered an intuitive and holistic cognitive process, in which various linguistic, contextual, and emotional cues are integrated to form a comprehensive understanding of the speaker’s true intention, which is argued not be limited to a step-by-step reasoning process. To verify this argument, we introduce a new prompting framework called SarcasmCue, which contains four prompting strategies, viz. chain of contradiction (CoC), graph of cues (GoC), bagging of cues (BoC) and tensor of cues (ToC), which elicits LLMs to detect human sarcasm by considering sequential and non-sequential prompting methods. Through a comprehensive empirical comparison on four benchmarking datasets, we show that the proposed four prompting methods outperforms standard IO prompting, CoT and ToT with a considerable margin, and non-sequential prompting generally outperforms sequential prompting.
摘要：阐述一系列中间推理步骤可以显著提高大语言模型解决复杂问题的能力，因为这样的步骤会唤起大语言模型的顺序思维。然而，人类对讽刺的理解通常被认为是一个直观的整体认知过程，在这个过程中，各种语言、语境和情感线索被整合在一起，形成对说话人真实意图的全面理解，这并不局限于一个循序渐进的推理过程。为了验证这一论点，我们引入了一个新的提示框架SarCasmCue，该框架包含四种提示策略，即提示策略、提示策略和提示策略。矛盾链(CoC)、线索图(GOC)、线索袋装(BoC)和线索张量(TOC)，引发LLMS通过考虑顺序和非顺序提示方法来检测人类讽刺。通过在四个基准数据集上的综合实证比较，我们发现所提出的四种提示方法的表现明显优于标准IO提示、COT和TOT，并且非顺序提示总体上优于顺序提示。

[NLP-5] Subgraph-Aware Training of Text-based Methods for Knowledge Graph Completion
[NLP-5] 基于文本的知识图完成方法的子图感知训练

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12703
作者: Youmin Ko,Hyemin Yang,Taeuk Kim,Hyunjoon Kim
关键词: pre-trained language models, Fine-tuning pre-trained language, knowledge graph completion, improve knowledge graph, language models
中文关键词: 预训练语言模型、微调预训练语言、知识图完成、改进知识图、语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 8 pages, including appendix with 8 figures and 12 tables, currently under open review for EMNLP 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Fine-tuning pre-trained language models (PLMs) has recently shown a potential to improve knowledge graph completion (KGC). However, most PLM-based methods encode only textual information, neglecting various topological structures of knowledge graphs (KGs). In this paper, we empirically validate the significant relations between the structural properties of KGs and the performance of the PLM-based methods. To leverage the structural knowledge, we propose a Subgraph-Aware Training framework for KGC (SATKGC) that combines (i) subgraph-aware mini-batching to encourage hard negative sampling, and (ii) a new contrastive learning method to focus more on harder entities and harder negative triples in terms of the structural properties. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study to comprehensively incorporate the structural inductive bias of the subgraphs into fine-tuning PLMs. Extensive experiments on four KGC benchmarks demonstrate the superiority of SATKGC. Our code is available.
摘要：微调预训练语言模型(PLM)最近显示出改善知识图完成(KGC)的潜力。然而，大多数基于PLM的方法只对文本信息进行编码，而忽略了知识图的各种拓扑结构。在本文中，我们实证地验证了KGS的结构特性与基于PLM的方法的性能之间的显著关系。为了利用结构知识，我们提出了一个子图感知的KGC训练框架(SATKGC)，该框架结合了(I)子图感知的小批处理来鼓励硬负抽样，以及(Ii)一种新的对比学习方法，从结构属性的角度更多地关注较难的实体和较硬的负三元组。据我们所知，这是第一次将子图的结构归纳偏差综合到微调PLM中的研究。在四个KGC基准上的大量实验证明了SATKGC的优越性。我们的代码是可用的。

[NLP-6] Reducing Biases towards Minoritized Populations in Medical Curricular Content via Artificial Intelligence for Fairer Health Outcomes
[NLP-6] 通过人工智能减少医学课程内容中对少数群体的偏见，以获得更公平的健康结果

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12680
作者: Chiman Salavati,Shannon Song,Willmar Sosa Diaz,Scott A. Hale,Roberto E. Montenegro,Fabricio Murai,Shiri Dori-Hacohen
关键词: Biased information, recently termed bisinformation, recently termed, Biased, bias
中文关键词: 有偏见的信息，最近被称为双信息，最近被称为，有偏见，偏见
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Under review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Biased information (recently termed bisinformation) continues to be taught in medical curricula, often long after having been debunked. In this paper, we introduce BRICC, a firstin-class initiative that seeks to mitigate medical bisinformation using machine learning to systematically identify and flag text with potential biases, for subsequent review in an expert-in-the-loop fashion, thus greatly accelerating an otherwise labor-intensive process. A gold-standard BRICC dataset was developed throughout several years, and contains over 12K pages of instructional materials. Medical experts meticulously annotated these documents for bias according to comprehensive coding guidelines, emphasizing gender, sex, age, geography, ethnicity, and race. Using this labeled dataset, we trained, validated, and tested medical bias classifiers. We test three classifier approaches: a binary type-specific classifier, a general bias classifier; an ensemble combining bias type-specific classifiers independently-trained; and a multitask learning (MTL) model tasked with predicting both general and type-specific biases. While MTL led to some improvement on race bias detection in terms of F1-score, it did not outperform binary classifiers trained specifically on each task. On general bias detection, the binary classifier achieves up to 0.923 of AUC, a 27.8% improvement over the baseline. This work lays the foundations for debiasing medical curricula by exploring a novel dataset and evaluating different training model strategies. Hence, it offers new pathways for more nuanced and effective mitigation of bisinformation.
摘要：有偏见的信息(最近被称为双信息)在医学课程中继续被教授，通常在被揭穿很久之后。在本文中，我们引入了BRICC，这是一种Firstin级的倡议，寻求使用机器学习来系统地识别和标记具有潜在偏见的文本，以便随后以专家在循环的方式进行审查，从而极大地加速了原本劳动密集型的过程。在几年的时间里开发了一个金标准的BRICC数据集，其中包含超过12000页的教学材料。医学专家根据全面的编码指南对这些文件进行了仔细的注释，强调了性别、性别、年龄、地理位置、种族和种族。使用这个标记的数据集，我们训练、验证和测试了医学偏向分类器。我们测试了三种分类器方法：二进制特定类型分类器，一般偏向分类器；集成独立训练的偏向特定分类器；以及多任务学习(MTL)模型，其任务是预测一般和特定类型的偏向。虽然MTL在F1分数方面导致了种族偏见检测的一些改进，但它的表现并不优于针对每个任务专门训练的二进制分类器。在一般偏差检测方面，二进制分类器获得了高达0.923的AUC值，比基线提高了27.8%。这项工作通过探索一个新的数据集和评估不同的训练模型策略，为消除医学课程的偏见奠定了基础。因此，它为更细微和有效地缓解双重信息提供了新的途径。

[NLP-7] Patch-Level Training for Large Language Models
[NLP-7] 大型语言模型的补丁级培训

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12665
作者: Chenze Shao,Fandong Meng,Jie Zhou
关键词: achieve remarkable progress, Large Language Models, Large Language, achieve remarkable, understanding and generation
中文关键词: 取得显着进展，大型语言模型，大型语言，取得显着的理解和生成
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve remarkable progress in language understanding and generation, their training efficiency has become a critical concern. Traditionally, LLMs are trained to predict the next token in a sequence. Despite the success of token-level training, it suffers from considerable computational costs due to the need to process an extensive number of tokens. To mitigate this issue, this paper introduces patch-level training for LLMs, which reduces the sequence length by compressing multiple tokens into a single patch. During patch-level training, we feed the language model shorter sequences of patches and train it to predict the next patch, thereby processing the majority of the training data at a significantly reduced computational cost. Following this, the model continues token-level training on the remaining training data to align with the inference mode. Experiments on a diverse range of models (370M-2.7B parameters) demonstrate that patch-level training can reduce overall computational costs to 0.5 \times , without compromising the model performance compared to token-level training. Source code: \urlthis https URL.
摘要：随着大语言模型在语言理解和生成方面取得了显著的进步，其训练效率已经成为一个重要的问题。传统上，LLM被训练来预测序列中的下一个令牌。尽管令牌级训练取得了成功，但由于需要处理大量的令牌，因此存在着相当大的计算成本。为了缓解这一问题，本文引入了补丁级别的LLMS训练，通过将多个令牌压缩到一个补丁中来减少序列长度。在补丁级别的训练中，我们向语言模型提供较短的补丁序列，并训练它预测下一个补丁，从而在显著降低计算成本的情况下处理大部分训练数据。在此之后，该模型继续对剩余的训练数据进行令牌级训练以与推理模式保持一致。在不同模型(370M-2.7B参数)上的实验表明，与令牌级训练相比，补丁级训练可以在不影响模型性能的情况下将总计算代价降低到0.5倍。源代码：\urlThis HTTPS URL。

[NLP-8] Domain-specific or Uncertainty-aware models: Does it really make a difference for biomedical text classification?
[NLP-8] 特定领域或不确定性感知模型：它真的会对生物医学文本分类产生影响吗？

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12626
作者: Aman Sinha,Timothee Mickus,Marianne Clausel,Mathieu Constant,Xavier Coubez
关键词: building domain-specific foundational, pretrained language models, domain-specific foundational models, NLP community, success of pretrained
中文关键词: 构建特定领域的基础、预训练的语言模型、特定领域的基础模型、NLP社区、预训练的成功
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: BioNLP 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The success of pretrained language models (PLMs) across a spate of use-cases has led to significant investment from the NLP community towards building domain-specific foundational models. On the other hand, in mission critical settings such as biomedical applications, other aspects also factor in-chief of which is a model’s ability to produce reasonable estimates of its own uncertainty. In the present study, we discuss these two desiderata through the lens of how they shape the entropy of a model’s output probability distribution. We find that domain specificity and uncertainty awareness can often be successfully combined, but the exact task at hand weighs in much more strongly.
摘要：预训练语言模型（PLM）在大量用例中的成功导致NLP社区对构建特定领域的基础模型进行了大量投资。另一方面，在生物医学应用等关键任务环境中，其他方面也是主要因素，其中模型对其自身不确定性进行合理估计的能力。在本研究中，我们通过这两个需求如何塑造模型输出概率分布的信息来讨论这两个需求。我们发现，领域特异性和不确定性意识通常可以成功结合，但手头的确切任务的分量要大得多。

[NLP-9] Harnessing the Power of Artificial Intelligence to Vitalize Endangered Indigenous Languages: Technologies and Experiences
[NLP-9] 利用人工智能的力量振兴濒危土著语言：技术和经验

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12620
作者: Claudio Pinhanez,Paulo Cavalin,Luciana Storto,Thomas Fimbow,Alexander Cobbinah,Julio Nogima,Marisa Vasconcelos,Pedro Domingues,Priscila de Souza Mizukami,Nicole Grell,Majoí Gongora,Isabel Gonçalves
关键词: Natural Language Processing, modern Natural Language, Artificial Intelligence, Large Language Models, exploring application areas
中文关键词: 自然语言处理、现代自然语言、人工智能、大型语言模型、探索应用领域
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Since 2022 we have been exploring application areas and technologies in which Artificial Intelligence (AI) and modern Natural Language Processing (NLP), such as Large Language Models (LLMs), can be employed to foster the usage and facilitate the documentation of Indigenous languages which are in danger of disappearing. We start by discussing the decreasing diversity of languages in the world and how working with Indigenous languages poses unique ethical challenges for AI and NLP. To address those challenges, we propose an alternative development AI cycle based on community engagement and usage. Then, we report encouraging results in the development of high-quality machine learning translators for Indigenous languages by fine-tuning state-of-the-art (SOTA) translators with tiny amounts of data and discuss how to avoid some common pitfalls in the process. We also present prototypes we have built in projects done in 2023 and 2024 with Indigenous communities in Brazil, aimed at facilitating writing, and discuss the development of Indigenous Language Models (ILMs) as a replicable and scalable way to create spell-checkers, next-word predictors, and similar tools. Finally, we discuss how we envision a future for language documentation where dying languages are preserved as interactive language models.
摘要：自2022年以来，我们一直在探索人工智能(AI)和现代自然语言处理(NLP)的应用领域和技术，例如大型语言模型(LLMS)，以促进濒临消失的土著语言的使用和文档编制。我们首先讨论世界上语言多样性的减少，以及与土著语言合作如何给人工智能和NLP带来独特的伦理挑战。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一个基于社区参与和使用的替代开发人工智能周期。然后，我们报告了通过使用少量数据微调最先进的(SOTA)翻译器来开发高质量的土著语言机器学习翻译器的令人鼓舞的结果，并讨论了如何避免过程中的一些常见陷阱。我们还介绍了我们在2023年和2024年与巴西土著社区共同开展的旨在促进写作的项目中建立的原型，并讨论了土著语言模型(ILM)的开发，以此作为创建拼写检查器、下一个单词预测器和类似工具的一种可复制和可扩展的方式。最后，我们讨论如何展望语言文档的未来，在那里，濒临死亡的语言将作为交互语言模型保留下来。

[NLP-10] AudienceView: AI-Assisted Interpretation of Audience Feedback in Journalism
[NLP-10] AudienceView：人工智能辅助解读新闻界观众反馈

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12613
作者: William Brannon,Doug Beeferman,Hang Jiang,Andrew Heyward,Deb Roy
关键词: impractically large volume, Understanding and making, audience comments online, volume of audience, important but difficult
中文关键词: 不切实际的大量，理解和制作，观众在线评论，观众量，重要但困难
类目: Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted at CSCW Demo 2024. 5 pages, 2 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Understanding and making use of audience feedback is important but difficult for journalists, who now face an impractically large volume of audience comments online. We introduce AudienceView, an online tool to help journalists categorize and interpret this feedback by leveraging large language models (LLMs). AudienceView identifies themes and topics, connects them back to specific comments, provides ways to visualize the sentiment and distribution of the comments, and helps users develop ideas for subsequent reporting projects. We consider how such tools can be useful in a journalist’s workflow, and emphasize the importance of contextual awareness and human judgment.
摘要：理解和利用观众反馈对于记者来说很重要，但也很困难，他们现在面临着大量不切实际的在线观众评论。我们引入AudienceView，这是一种在线工具，可帮助记者通过利用大型语言模型（LLM）对这些反馈进行分类和解释。AudienceView识别主题和主题，将它们与特定评论联系起来，提供可视化评论的情绪和分布的方法，并帮助用户为后续报告项目开发想法。我们考虑这些工具如何在记者的工作流程中发挥作用，并强调上下文意识和人类判断的重要性。

[NLP-11] E5-V: Universal Embeddings with Multimodal Large Language Models
[NLP-11] E5-V：具有多模式大型语言模型的通用嵌入

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12580
作者: Ting Jiang,Minghui Song,Zihan Zhang,Haizhen Huang,Weiwei Deng,Feng Sun,Qi Zhang,Deqing Wang,Fuzhen Zhuang
关键词: shown promising advancements, Multimodal large language, large language models, language understanding, large language
中文关键词: 显示出有希望的进步，多模式大型语言、大型语言模型、语言理解、大型语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Information Retrieval (cs.IR)
备注: Code and models are available at this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown promising advancements in general visual and language understanding. However, the representation of multimodal information using MLLMs remains largely unexplored. In this work, we introduce a new framework, E5-V, designed to adapt MLLMs for achieving universal multimodal embeddings. Our findings highlight the significant potential of MLLMs in representing multimodal inputs compared to previous approaches. By leveraging MLLMs with prompts, E5-V effectively bridges the modality gap between different types of inputs, demonstrating strong performance in multimodal embeddings even without fine-tuning. We propose a single modality training approach for E5-V, where the model is trained exclusively on text pairs. This method demonstrates significant improvements over traditional multimodal training on image-text pairs, while reducing training costs by approximately 95%. Additionally, this approach eliminates the need for costly multimodal training data collection. Extensive experiments across four types of tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of E5-V. As a universal multimodal model, E5-V not only achieves but often surpasses state-of-the-art performance in each task, despite being trained on a single modality.
然而，使用MLLMS来表示多模式信息在很大程度上还没有被探索。通过利用带提示的MLLMS，E5-V有效地弥合了不同类型输入之间的通道差距，即使在没有微调的情况下，也在多通道嵌入中表现出强大的性能。此外，这种方法消除了收集昂贵的多模式培训数据的需要。四种任务的广泛实验证明了E5-V的有效性。作为一种通用的多模式模型，E5-V不仅在每项任务中实现了最先进的性能，而且往往超过了最先进的性能，尽管接受了单一模式的培训。

[NLP-12] Abstraction Alignment: Comparing Model and Human Conceptual Relationships
[NLP-12] 抽象对齐：比较模型和人类概念关系

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12543
作者: Angie Boggust,Hyemin Bang,Hendrik Strobelt,Arvind Satyanarayan
关键词: people efficiently process, broad reusable patterns, Abstraction, efficiently process, reusable patterns
中文关键词: 人们高效处理，广泛的可重用模式，抽象，高效处理，可重用模式
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC)
备注: 14 pages, 3 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Abstraction – the process of generalizing specific examples into broad reusable patterns – is central to how people efficiently process and store information and apply their knowledge to new data. Promisingly, research has shown that ML models learn representations that span levels of abstraction, from specific concepts like “bolo tie” and “car tire” to more general concepts like “CEO” and “model”. However, existing techniques analyze these representations in isolation, treating learned concepts as independent artifacts rather than an interconnected web of abstraction. As a result, although we can identify the concepts a model uses to produce its output, it is difficult to assess if it has learned a human-aligned abstraction of the concepts that will generalize to new data. To address this gap, we introduce abstraction alignment, a methodology to measure the agreement between a model’s learned abstraction and the expected human abstraction. We quantify abstraction alignment by comparing model outputs against a human abstraction graph, such as linguistic relationships or medical disease hierarchies. In evaluation tasks interpreting image models, benchmarking language models, and analyzing medical datasets, abstraction alignment provides a deeper understanding of model behavior and dataset content, differentiating errors based on their agreement with human knowledge, expanding the verbosity of current model quality metrics, and revealing ways to improve existing human abstractions.
摘要：抽象–将特定示例概括为广泛的可重用模式的过程–是人们高效地处理和存储信息并将他们的知识应用于新数据的核心。令人振奋的是，研究表明，ML模型学习的表示法跨越了不同的抽象层次，从特定的概念，如“波罗领带”和“汽车轮胎”，到更一般的概念，如“首席执行官”和“模型”。然而，现有的技术孤立地分析这些表示，将学习的概念视为独立的构件，而不是相互连接的抽象网络。因此，尽管我们可以确定模型用于产生其输出的概念，但很难评估它是否已经学习了将概括为新数据的概念的人类一致的抽象。为了解决这一差距，我们引入了抽象对齐，这是一种度量模型的学习抽象和预期的人类抽象之间的一致性的方法。我们通过将模型输出与人类抽象图(如语言关系或医疗疾病等级)进行比较来量化抽象对齐。在解释图像模型、基准语言模型和分析医学数据集的评估任务中，抽象对齐提供了对模型行为和数据集内容的更深层次的理解，基于它们与人类知识的一致性来区分错误，扩展了当前模型质量度量的冗长，并揭示了改进现有人类抽象的方法。

[NLP-13] owards Collaborative Intelligence: Propagating Intentions and Reasoning for Multi-Agent Coordination with Large Language Models
[NLP-13] owards协作智能：利用大型语言模型进行多智能体协调的意图和推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12532
作者: Xihe Qiu,Haoyu Wang,Xiaoyu Tan,Chao Qu,Yujie Xiong,Yuan Cheng,Yinghui Xu,Wei Chu,Yuan Qi
关键词: Effective collaboration, systems requires communicating, agents, Effective, multi-agent systems requires
中文关键词: 有效的协作，系统需要沟通，代理，有效的多代理系统需要
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Effective collaboration in multi-agent systems requires communicating goals and intentions between agents. Current agent frameworks often suffer from dependencies on single-agent execution and lack robust inter-module communication, frequently leading to suboptimal multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) policies and inadequate task coordination. To address these challenges, we present a framework for training large language models (LLMs) as collaborative agents to enable coordinated behaviors in cooperative MARL. Each agent maintains a private intention consisting of its current goal and associated sub-tasks. Agents broadcast their intentions periodically, allowing other agents to infer coordination tasks. A propagation network transforms broadcast intentions into teammate-specific communication messages, sharing relevant goals with designated teammates. The architecture of our framework is structured into planning, grounding, and execution modules. During execution, multiple agents interact in a downstream environment and communicate intentions to enable coordinated behaviors. The grounding module dynamically adapts comprehension strategies based on emerging coordination patterns, while feedback from execution agents influnces the planning module, enabling the dynamic re-planning of sub-tasks. Results in collaborative environment simulation demonstrate intention propagation reduces miscoordination errors by aligning sub-task dependencies between agents. Agents learn when to communicate intentions and which teammates require task details, resulting in emergent coordinated behaviors. This demonstrates the efficacy of intention sharing for cooperative multi-agent RL based on LLMs.
摘要：多智能体系统中的有效协作需要多个智能体之间进行目标和意图的沟通。当前的代理框架往往依赖于单代理的执行，缺乏健壮的模块间通信，经常导致次优的多代理强化学习(MAIL)策略和不充分的任务协调。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一个框架，用于将大型语言模型(LLM)训练为协作代理，以使协作MAIL中的协调行为成为可能。每个代理维护由其当前目标和关联子任务组成的私人意图。代理定期广播他们的意图，允许其他代理推断协调任务。传播网络将广播意图转换为特定于队友的通信消息，与指定的队友共享相关目标。我们框架的体系结构分为计划模块、基础模块和执行模块。在执行过程中，多个代理在下游环境中交互，并传达意图以实现协调行为。基础模块根据新出现的协调模式动态调整理解策略，而来自执行代理的反馈影响规划模块，使子任务的动态重新规划成为可能。协同环境下的仿真结果表明，意图传播通过调整智能体之间的子任务依赖关系来减少不协调错误。代理学习何时传达意图以及哪些队友需要任务细节，从而产生紧急协调行为。这证明了基于LLMS的协作多智能体RL意图共享的有效性。

[NLP-14] Crafting the Path: Robust Query Rewriting for Information Retrieval
[NLP-14] 制定路径：信息检索的稳健查询重写

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12529
作者: Ingeol Baek,Jimin Lee,Joonho Yang,Hwanhee Lee
关键词: Large Language Models, Query rewriting aims, Crafting the Path, Query rewriting, Query
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、查询重写目标、制定路径、查询重写、查询
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 1 figure, 12 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Query rewriting aims to generate a new query that can complement the original query to improve the information retrieval system. Recent studies on query rewriting, such as query2doc (Q2D), query2expand (Q2E) and querey2cot (Q2C), rely on the internal knowledge of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate a relevant passage to add information to the query. Nevertheless, the efficacy of these methodologies may markedly decline in instances where the requisite knowledge is not encapsulated within the model’s intrinsic parameters. In this paper, we propose a novel structured query rewriting method called Crafting the Path tailored for retrieval systems. Crafting the Path involves a three-step process that crafts query-related information necessary for finding the passages to be searched in each step. Specifically, the Crafting the Path begins with Query Concept Comprehension, proceeds to Query Type Identification, and finally conducts Expected Answer Extraction. Experimental results show that our method outperforms previous rewriting methods, especially in less familiar domains for LLMs. We demonstrate that our method is less dependent on the internal parameter knowledge of the model and generates queries with fewer factual inaccuracies. Furthermore, we observe that Crafting the Path has less latency compared to the baselines.
摘要：查询重写的目的是生成一个能够补充原有查询的新查询，以改进信息检索系统。最近关于查询重写的研究，如query2doc(Q2D)、query2Expand(Q2E)和querey2cot(Q2C)，依赖于大语言模型(LLMS)的内部知识来生成相关段落来向查询添加信息。然而，在必要的知识没有包含在模型的内在参数中的情况下，这些方法的有效性可能会显著下降。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的结构化查询重写方法，称为为检索系统定制的定制路径。创建路径涉及三个步骤的过程，即在每个步骤中创建查找要搜索的段落所需的与查询相关的信息。具体地说，路径的构建从查询概念理解开始，进行到查询类型识别，最后进行预期答案提取。实验结果表明，我们的方法优于以往的重写方法，特别是在不太熟悉的领域。我们证明了我们的方法对模型的内部参数知识的依赖性较小，并且生成的查询具有较少的事实不准确性。此外，我们观察到，与基线相比，精心设计路径的延迟更小。

[NLP-15] Struct-X: Enhancing Large Language Models Reasoning with Structured Data
[NLP-15] Struct-X：用结构化数据增强大型语言模型推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12522
作者: Xiaoyu Tan,Haoyu Wang,Xihe Qiu,Yuan Cheng,Yinghui Xu,Wei Chu,Yuan Qi
关键词: large language models, Structured data, rich in logical, language models, logical and relational
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、结构化数据、丰富逻辑、语言模型、逻辑和关系
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Structured data, rich in logical and relational information, has the potential to enhance the reasoning abilities of large language models (LLMs). Still, its integration poses a challenge due to the risk of overwhelming LLMs with excessive tokens and irrelevant context information. To address this, we propose Struct-X, a novel framework that operates through five key phases: ``read-model-fill-reflect-reason’’ efficiently enabling LLMs to utilize structured data. It begins by encoding structured data into a topological space using graph embeddings, followed by filling in missing entity information with knowledge retrieval modules, and filtering out irrelevant tokens via a self-supervised module. The final phase involves constructing a topological network with selected tokens to further reduce the total token length for more effective LLM inference. Additionally, Struct-X includes an Auxiliary Module trained to generate prompts, aiding LLMs in analyzing structured data. Extensive experiments on benchmarks, including the knowledge graph question-answer task and the long document reading comprehension task, show that Struct-X notably improves LLM reasoning, demonstrating the effectiveness of structured data augmentation in improving LLM inference with complex input context.
摘要：结构化数据蕴含着丰富的逻辑和关系信息，有可能提高大型语言模型的推理能力。尽管如此，它的整合仍然构成了一个挑战，因为存在用过多的令牌和不相关的上下文信息压倒LLM的风险。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了Struct-X，这是一个新的框架，通过五个关键阶段进行操作：“读取-模型-填充-反映-原因”，有效地使LLM能够利用结构化数据。它首先使用图嵌入将结构化数据编码到拓扑空间中，然后使用知识检索模块填充缺失的实体信息，并通过自我监督模块过滤掉不相关的令牌。最后一个阶段是使用选定的令牌构建一个拓扑网络，以进一步减少令牌的总长度，从而更有效地进行LLM推理。此外，Struct-X包括一个经过培训的辅助模块，用于生成提示，帮助LLMS分析结构化数据。在知识图谱问答任务和长文档阅读理解任务等基准测试上的大量实验表明，Struct-X显著地改进了LLM推理，证明了结构化数据增强在复杂输入上下文下改进LLM推理的有效性。

[NLP-16] On Initializing Transformers with Pre-trained Embeddings
[NLP-16] 关于使用预先训练的嵌入式初始化变形金刚

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12514
作者: Ha Young Kim,Niranjan Balasubramanian,Byungkon Kang
关键词: transformer based models, random initialization schemes, training transformer based, common practice, transformer based
中文关键词: 基于Transformer的模型、随机初始化方案、基于Transformer的训练、常见实践、基于Transformer的
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:It has become common practice now to use random initialization schemes, rather than the pre-trained embeddings, when training transformer based models from scratch. Indeed, we find that pre-trained word embeddings from GloVe, and some sub-word embeddings extracted from language models such as T5 and mT5 fare much worse compared to random initialization. This is counter-intuitive given the well-known representational and transfer-learning advantages of pre-training. Interestingly, we also find that BERT and mBERT embeddings fare better than random initialization, showing the advantages of pre-trained representations. In this work, we posit two potential factors that contribute to these mixed results: the model sensitivity to parameter distribution and the embedding interactions with position encodings. We observe that pre-trained GloVe, T5, and mT5 embeddings have a wider distribution of values. As argued in the initialization studies, such large value initializations can lead to poor training because of saturated outputs. Further, the larger embedding values can, in effect, absorb the smaller position encoding values when added together, thus losing position information. Standardizing the pre-trained embeddings to a narrow range (e.g. as prescribed by Xavier) leads to substantial gains for Glove, T5, and mT5 embeddings. On the other hand, BERT pre-trained embeddings, while larger, are still relatively closer to Xavier initialization range which may allow it to effectively transfer the pre-trained knowledge.
摘要：在从头开始训练基于变压器的模型时，使用随机初始化方案而不是预先训练的嵌入已经成为一种普遍的做法。事实上，我们发现，从手套中提取的预训练单词嵌入以及从T5和MT5等语言模型中提取的一些子词嵌入比随机初始化要差得多。考虑到预训众所周知的代表性和迁移学习优势，这是有悖于直觉的。有趣的是，我们还发现BERT和mBERT嵌入比随机初始化表现得更好，显示了预训练表示的优势。在这项工作中，我们假设了导致这些混合结果的两个潜在因素：模型对参数分布的敏感性和嵌入与位置编码的相互作用。我们观察到，预先训练的手套、T5和MT5嵌入具有更广泛的值分布。正如初始化研究中所指出的，由于输出饱和，如此大的值初始化可能会导致较差的训练。此外，当加在一起时，较大的嵌入值实际上可以吸收较小的位置编码值，从而丢失位置信息。将预先训练的嵌入标准化到较窄的范围(例如，如Xavier所规定的)将导致手套、T5和MT5嵌入的显著收益。另一方面，BERT的预训练嵌入虽然较大，但仍然相对接近Xavier的初始化范围，这可能使其能够有效地传递预训练的知识。

[NLP-17] textitGeoHard: Towards Measuring Class-wise Hardness through Modelling Class Semantics
[NLP-17] textitGeohard：通过建模类语义来衡量类硬度

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12512
作者: Fengyu Cai,Xinran Zhao,Hongming Zhang,Iryna Gurevych,Heinz Koeppl
关键词: Recent advances, textit, low-resource scenarios, sample selection, selection within low-resource
中文关键词: 最新进展、文本、低资源场景、样本选择、低资源内的选择
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Findings of ACL 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advances in measuring hardness-wise properties of data guide language models in sample selection within low-resource scenarios. However, class-specific properties are overlooked for task setup and learning. How will these properties influence model learning and is it generalizable across datasets? To answer this question, this work formally initiates the concept of \textitclass-wise hardness . Experiments across eight natural language understanding (NLU) datasets demonstrate a consistent hardness distribution across learning paradigms, models, and human judgment. Subsequent experiments unveil a notable challenge in measuring such class-wise hardness with instance-level metrics in previous works. To address this, we propose \textitGeoHard for class-wise hardness measurement by modeling class geometry in the semantic embedding space. \textitGeoHard surpasses instance-level metrics by over 59 percent on \textitPearson 's correlation on measuring class-wise hardness. Our analysis theoretically and empirically underscores the generality of \textitGeoHard as a fresh perspective on data diagnosis. Additionally, we showcase how understanding class-wise hardness can practically aid in improving task learning.
摘要：在低资源情景下，在样本选择中测量数据指导语言模型的硬度特性方面的最新进展。但是，对于任务设置和学习，会忽略特定于类的属性。这些属性将如何影响模型学习，以及它是否可跨数据集推广？为了回答这个问题，这项工作正式提出了类硬度的概念。在八个自然语言理解(NLU)数据集上的实验表明，学习范例、模型和人类判断之间的硬度分布一致。随后的实验揭示了在以前的工作中使用实例级度量来测量这种类级硬度方面的一个显着挑战。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了通过在语义嵌入空间中对类几何进行建模来进行类硬度度量的文本GeoHard。我们的分析从理论和经验上强调了GeoHard作为数据诊断的一个新视角的普遍性。此外，我们还展示了理解类硬度实际上如何有助于改进任务学习。

[NLP-18] MERLIN: Multimodal Embedding Refinement via LLM-based Iterative Navigation for Text-Video Retrieval-Rerank Pipeline
[NLP-18] 梅林：通过基于LLM的迭代导航进行多模式嵌入细化文本-视频检索-Rerank管道

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12508
作者: Donghoon Han,Eunhwan Park,Gisang Lee,Adam Lee,Nojun Kwak
关键词: collections increasingly challenging, made accurately retrieving, accurately retrieving relevant, large collections increasingly, retrieving relevant videos
中文关键词: 收藏越来越具有挑战性，准确检索，准确检索相关的大型收藏，检索相关的视频
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: Work in progress

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The rapid expansion of multimedia content has made accurately retrieving relevant videos from large collections increasingly challenging. Recent advancements in text-video retrieval have focused on cross-modal interactions, large-scale foundation model training, and probabilistic modeling, yet often neglect the crucial user perspective, leading to discrepancies between user queries and the content retrieved. To address this, we introduce MERLIN (Multimodal Embedding Refinement via LLM-based Iterative Navigation), a novel, training-free pipeline that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for iterative feedback learning. MERLIN refines query embeddings from a user perspective, enhancing alignment between queries and video content through a dynamic question answering process. Experimental results on datasets like MSR-VTT, MSVD, and ActivityNet demonstrate that MERLIN substantially improves Recall@1, outperforming existing systems and confirming the benefits of integrating LLMs into multimodal retrieval systems for more responsive and context-aware multimedia retrieval.
摘要：多媒体内容的快速膨胀使得从海量集合中准确检索相关视频变得越来越困难。文本-视频检索的最新进展集中在跨模式交互、大规模基础模型训练和概率建模上，但往往忽略了关键的用户视角，导致用户查询和检索的内容之间存在差异。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了Merlin(基于LLM的迭代导航的多模式嵌入精化)，这是一个新的、无需训练的管道，它利用大型语言模型(LLM)进行迭代反馈学习。Merlin从用户的角度改进了查询嵌入，通过动态问答过程增强了查询和视频内容之间的一致性。在MSR-VTT、MSVD和ActivityNet等数据集上的实验结果表明，Merlin显著提高了Recall@1，性能优于现有系统，并证实了将LLMS集成到多模式检索系统中以实现更具响应性和上下文感知的多媒体检索的好处。

[NLP-19] Case2Code: Learning Inductive Reasoning with Synthetic Data
[NLP-19] Case 2Code：使用合成数据学习归纳推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12504
作者: Yunfan Shao,Linyang Li,Yichuan Ma,Peiji Li,Demin Song,Qinyuan Cheng,Shimin Li,Xiaonan Li,Pengyu Wang,Qipeng Guo,Hang Yan,Xipeng Qiu,Xuanjing Huang,Dahua Lin
关键词: large language models, impressive ability shown, Complex reasoning, language models, impressive ability
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，表现出令人印象深刻的能力，复杂推理，语言模型，令人印象深刻的能力
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Complex reasoning is an impressive ability shown by large language models (LLMs). Most LLMs are skilled in deductive reasoning, such as chain-of-thought prompting or iterative tool-using to solve challenging tasks step-by-step. In this paper, we hope to focus on evaluating and teaching LLMs to conduct inductive reasoning, that is, LLMs are supposed to infer underlying rules by observing examples or sequential transformations. However, collecting large-scale and diverse human-generated inductive data is challenging. We focus on data synthesis in the code domain and propose a \textbfCase2Code task by exploiting the expressiveness and correctness of programs. Specifically, we collect a diverse set of executable programs, synthesize input-output transformations for each program, and force LLMs to infer the underlying code implementations based on the synthetic I/O cases. We first evaluate representative LLMs on the synthesized Case2Code task and demonstrate that the Case-to-code induction is challenging for LLMs. Then, we synthesize large-scale Case2Code training samples to train LLMs to perform inductive reasoning. Experimental results show that such induction training benefits not only in distribution Case2Code performance but also enhances various coding abilities of trained LLMs, demonstrating the great potential of learning inductive reasoning via synthetic data.
摘要：复杂推理是大型语言模型所展示的一种令人印象深刻的能力。大多数LLM擅长演绎推理，如思维链式提示或迭代工具–一步一步地解决具有挑战性的任务。在本文中，我们希望重点评价和教导LLM进行归纳推理，即LLM应该通过观察实例或顺序转换来推断潜在的规则。然而，收集大规模和多样化的人类产生的归纳数据是具有挑战性的。我们将重点放在代码域的数据合成上，通过利用程序的表现力和正确性，提出了一个\extbfCase2Code任务。具体地说，我们收集了一组不同的可执行程序，为每个程序合成输入-输出转换，并迫使LLM基于合成的I/O情况推断底层代码实现。我们首先在合成的Case2Code任务上评估了典型的LLM，并证明了案例到代码的归纳对于LLMS来说是具有挑战性的。然后，我们合成大规模的Case2Code训练样本来训练LLM进行归纳推理。实验结果表明，这种归纳训练不仅有利于提高Case2Code的分布性能，而且提高了训练后的LLM的各种编码能力，展示了通过合成数据学习归纳推理的巨大潜力。

[NLP-20] Automate or Assist? The Role of Computational Models in Identifying Gendered Discourse in US Capital Trial Transcripts
[NLP-20] 自动化还是辅助？计算模型在识别美国资本审判笔录中性别话语中的作用

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12500
作者: Andrea W Wen-Yi,Kathryn Adamson,Nathalie Greenfield,Rachel Goldberg,Sandra Babcock,David Mimno,Allison Koenecke
关键词: studied for biases, courtroom actors, actors in criminal, long been studied, computational models
中文关键词: 研究偏见、法庭行为者、犯罪行为者、长期研究、计算模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The language used by US courtroom actors in criminal trials has long been studied for biases. However, systematic studies for bias in high-stakes court trials have been difficult, due to the nuanced nature of bias and the legal expertise required. New large language models offer the possibility to automate annotation, saving time and cost. But validating these approaches requires both high quantitative performance as well as an understanding of how automated methods fit in existing workflows, and what they really offer. In this paper we present a case study of adding an automated system to a complex and high-stakes problem: identifying gender-biased language in US capital trials for women defendants. Our team of experienced death-penalty lawyers and NLP technologists pursued a three-phase study: first annotating manually, then training and evaluating computational models, and finally comparing human annotations to model predictions. Unlike many typical NLP tasks, annotating for gender bias in months-long capital trials was a complicated task that involves with many individual judgment calls. In contrast to standard arguments for automation that are based on efficiency and scalability, legal experts found the computational models most useful in challenging their personal bias in annotation and providing opportunities to refine and build consensus on rules for annotation. This suggests that seeking to replace experts with computational models is both unrealistic and undesirable. Rather, computational models offer valuable opportunities to assist the legal experts in annotation-based studies.
摘要：长期以来，人们对美国法庭人员在刑事审判中使用的语言存在偏见进行了研究。然而，由于偏见的微妙性质和所需的法律专业知识，对高风险法庭审判中的偏见进行系统研究一直是困难的。新的大型语言模型提供了自动化注释的可能性，从而节省了时间和成本。但是，验证这些方法既需要高的量化性能，也需要了解自动化方法如何适应现有的工作流，以及它们真正提供了什么。在这篇文章中，我们提出了一个案例研究，为一个复杂和高风险的问题添加了一个自动化系统：在美国对女性被告的死刑审判中识别性别偏见的语言。我们由经验丰富的死刑律师和NLP技术专家组成的团队进行了一项分三个阶段的研究：首先手动注释，然后训练和评估计算模型，最后将人类注释与模型预测进行比较。与许多典型的NLP任务不同，在长达数月的首都审判中，为性别偏见添加注释是一项复杂的任务，涉及许多个人判断电话。与基于效率和可伸缩性的自动化标准论点不同，法律专家发现，计算模型在挑战他们在注释方面的个人偏见方面最有用，并提供了完善注释规则并就其达成共识的机会。这表明，寻求用计算模型取代专家既不现实，也不可取。相反，计算模型提供了宝贵的机会来帮助法律专家进行基于注释的研究。

[NLP-21] Evaluating Linguistic Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs in the Lens of Few-Shot Learning
[NLP-21] 从少镜头学习的角度评估多模式LLM的语言能力

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12498
作者: Mustafa Dogan,Ilker Kesen,Iacer Calixto,Aykut Erdem,Erkut Erdem
关键词: Multimodal Large Language, Multimodal Large, Large Language Models, capabilities of Multimodal, Large Language
中文关键词: 多模式大型语言，多模式大型语言，大型语言模型，多模式，大型语言的能力
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: Preprint. 33 pages, 17 Figures, 3 Tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The linguistic capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are critical for their effective application across diverse tasks. This study aims to evaluate the performance of MLLMs on the VALSE benchmark, focusing on the efficacy of few-shot In-Context Learning (ICL), and Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting. We conducted a comprehensive assessment of state-of-the-art MLLMs, varying in model size and pretraining datasets. The experimental results reveal that ICL and CoT prompting significantly boost model performance, particularly in tasks requiring complex reasoning and contextual understanding. Models pretrained on captioning datasets show superior zero-shot performance, while those trained on interleaved image-text data benefit from few-shot learning. Our findings provide valuable insights into optimizing MLLMs for better grounding of language in visual contexts, highlighting the importance of the composition of pretraining data and the potential of few-shot learning strategies to improve the reasoning abilities of MLLMs.
摘要：多通道大型语言模型的语言能力对于其在不同任务中的有效应用至关重要。本研究的目的是在Valse基准测试中评估MLLMS的性能，重点关注少量情景学习(ICL)和思想链(COT)提示的效果。我们对最新的最大似然模型进行了全面的评估，评估的模型大小和预训练数据集各不相同。实验结果表明，ICL和COT提示显著提高了模型的性能，特别是在需要复杂推理和上下文理解的任务中。在字幕数据集上预先训练的模型显示出卓越的零镜头性能，而那些在交错图文数据上训练的模型则受益于少镜头学习。我们的发现为优化MLLMS以更好地在视觉环境中奠定语言基础提供了有价值的见解，强调了预训练数据组合的重要性和少数几次学习策略提高MLLMS推理能力的潜力。

[NLP-22] Pretraining Data and Tokenizer for Indic LLM
[NLP-22] 印度LLM的预训练数据和代币器

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12481
作者: Rahul Kumar,Shubham Kakde,Divyansh Rajput,Daud Ibrahim,Rishabh Nahata,Pidathala Sowjanya,Deepak Kumar
关键词: Indic large language, Indic, Common Crawl, including Common Crawl, Common Crawl data
中文关键词: 印度大型语言、印度、Common Crawl，包括Common Crawl、Common Crawl数据
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present a novel approach to data preparation for developing multilingual Indic large language model. Our meticulous data acquisition spans open-source and proprietary sources, including Common Crawl, Indic books, news articles, and Wikipedia, ensuring a diverse and rich linguistic representation. For each Indic language, we design a custom preprocessing pipeline to effectively eliminate redundant and low-quality text content. Additionally, we perform deduplication on Common Crawl data to address the redundancy present in 70% of the crawled web pages. This study focuses on developing high-quality data, optimizing tokenization for our multilingual dataset for Indic large language models with 3B and 7B parameters, engineered for superior performance in Indic languages. We introduce a novel multilingual tokenizer training strategy, demonstrating our custom-trained Indic tokenizer outperforms the state-of-the-art OpenAI Tiktoken tokenizer, achieving a superior token-to-word ratio for Indic languages.
摘要：我们提出了一种新的数据准备方法，用于开发多语言的印度语大语言模型。我们细致的数据获取跨越开源和专有来源，包括Common Crawl、印度书籍、新闻文章和维基百科，确保了多样化和丰富的语言表示。对于每种印度语，我们设计了一条定制的预处理流水线，有效地消除了冗余和低质量的文本内容。此外，我们对常见爬行数据执行重复数据删除，以解决70%的爬行网页中存在的冗余。这项研究的重点是开发高质量的数据，为我们的多语言数据集优化标记化，用于具有3B和7B参数的印度大型语言模型，旨在实现印度语言的卓越性能。我们介绍了一种新的多语言标记器训练策略，演示了我们的定制训练的印度语标记器的性能优于最先进的OpenAI TikToken标记器，实现了印度语优异的词义比。

[NLP-23] A Novel Dependency Framework for Enhancing Discourse Data Analysis
[NLP-23] 增强话语数据分析的新型依赖性框架

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12473
作者: Kun Sun,Rong Wang
关键词: discourse corpora based, discourse corpora, discourse, PDTB, dependency
中文关键词: 基于话语库、话语库、话语、PDTB、依赖性
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The development of different theories of discourse structure has led to the establishment of discourse corpora based on these theories. However, the existence of discourse corpora established on different theoretical bases creates challenges when it comes to exploring them in a consistent and cohesive way. This study has as its primary focus the conversion of PDTB annotations into dependency structures. It employs refined BERT-based discourse parsers to test the validity of the dependency data derived from the PDTB-style corpora in English, Chinese, and several other languages. By converting both PDTB and RST annotations for the same texts into dependencies, this study also applies ``dependency distance’’ metrics to examine the correlation between RST dependencies and PDTB dependencies in English. The results show that the PDTB dependency data is valid and that there is a strong correlation between the two types of dependency distance. This study presents a comprehensive approach for analyzing and evaluating discourse corpora by employing discourse dependencies to achieve unified analysis. By applying dependency representations, we can extract data from PDTB, RST, and SDRT corpora in a coherent and unified manner. Moreover, the cross-linguistic validation establishes the framework’s generalizability beyond English. The establishment of this comprehensive dependency framework overcomes limitations of existing discourse corpora, supporting a diverse range of algorithms and facilitating further studies in computational discourse analysis and language sciences.
摘要：不同的语篇结构理论的发展导致了以这些理论为基础的语篇语料库的建立。然而，建立在不同理论基础上的语篇语料库的存在给以一致和连贯的方式探索它们带来了挑战。这项研究的主要焦点是将PDTB注释转换为依存结构。它使用改进的基于BERT的语篇分析器来测试从PDTB风格的英语、汉语和其他几种语言的语料库中获得的依存数据的有效性。通过将同一篇文章的PDTB和RST标注转换为依存关系，本研究还运用“依存距离”度量来检验英语中RST依存关系和PDTB依存关系之间的关系。结果表明，PDTB依赖数据是有效的，并且两种依赖距离之间存在较强的相关性。本研究提出了一种综合分析和评价语篇语料库的方法，即利用语篇依存关系来实现统一分析。通过应用依存关系表示，我们可以以一致和统一的方式从PDTB、RST和SDRT语料库中提取数据。此外，跨语言验证确立了该框架在英语之外的泛化能力。这一全面依存关系框架的建立克服了现有语料库的局限性，支持多种算法，促进了计算语篇分析和语言科学的进一步研究。

[NLP-24] Characterization of Political Polarized Users Attacked by Language Toxicity on Twitter
[NLP-24] Twitter上受到语言毒性攻击的政治两极分化用户的描述

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12471
作者: Wentao Xu
关键词: presidential elections, Understanding the dynamics, social media, propagation of misinformation, development of echo
中文关键词: 总统选举、了解动态、社交媒体、错误信息的传播、回声的发展
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: This work has been accepted by 2024 Conference on Computer Supported Cooperative Work and Social Computing (CSCW2024). Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), New York, NY, USA

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Understanding the dynamics of language toxicity on social media is important for us to investigate the propagation of misinformation and the development of echo chambers for political scenarios such as U.S. presidential elections. Recent research has used large-scale data to investigate the dynamics across social media platforms. However, research on the toxicity dynamics is not enough. This study aims to provide a first exploration of the potential language toxicity flow among Left, Right and Center users. Specifically, we aim to examine whether Left users were easier to be attacked by language toxicity. In this study, more than 500M Twitter posts were examined. It was discovered that Left users received much more toxic replies than Right and Center users.
摘要：了解社交媒体上语言毒性的动态对于我们调查错误信息的传播和美国总统选举等政治场景回音室的发展非常重要。最近的研究使用大规模数据来调查社交媒体平台的动态。然而，对毒性动力学的研究还不够。这项研究旨在首次探索左、右和中用户之间潜在的语言毒性流。具体来说，我们的目标是检查Left用户是否更容易受到语言毒性的攻击。在这项研究中，检查了超过5亿条Twitter帖子。结果发现，Left用户收到的有毒回复比Right和Central用户多得多。

[NLP-25] Continual Learning for Temporal-Sensitive Question Answering
[NLP-25] 持续学习以实现时间敏感问题解答

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12470
作者: Wanqi Yang,Yunqiu Xu,Yanda Li,Kunze Wang,Binbin Huang,Ling Chen
关键词: Sensitive Question Answering, Temporal Sensitive Question, Question Answering, Sensitive Question, Temporal Sensitive
中文关键词: 敏感问题解答，时间敏感问题，问题解答，敏感问题，时间敏感
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted by IJCNN 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this study, we explore an emerging research area of Continual Learning for Temporal Sensitive Question Answering (CLTSQA). Previous research has primarily focused on Temporal Sensitive Question Answering (TSQA), often overlooking the unpredictable nature of future events. In real-world applications, it’s crucial for models to continually acquire knowledge over time, rather than relying on a static, complete dataset. Our paper investigates strategies that enable models to adapt to the ever-evolving information landscape, thereby addressing the challenges inherent in CLTSQA. To support our research, we first create a novel dataset, divided into five subsets, designed specifically for various stages of continual learning. We then propose a training framework for CLTSQA that integrates temporal memory replay and temporal contrastive learning. Our experimental results highlight two significant insights: First, the CLTSQA task introduces unique challenges for existing models. Second, our proposed framework effectively navigates these challenges, resulting in improved performance.
摘要：在这项研究中，我们探索了一个新的研究领域–时间敏感问题的持续学习(CLTSQA)。以前的研究主要集中在时间敏感的问题回答(TSQA)上，往往忽略了未来事件的不可预测性。在现实世界的应用程序中，模型随着时间的推移不断获取知识是至关重要的，而不是依赖于静态的、完整的数据集。我们的论文研究了使模型能够适应不断发展的信息环境，从而解决CLTSQA固有的挑战的策略。为了支持我们的研究，我们首先创建了一个新的数据集，分为五个子集，专门为持续学习的不同阶段设计。在此基础上，我们提出了一种集成了时间记忆回放和时间对比学习的CLTSQA训练框架。我们的实验结果突出了两个重要的见解：首先，CLTSQA任务为现有模型引入了独特的挑战。其次，我们建议的框架有效地应对了这些挑战，从而提高了性能。

[NLP-26] Across Platforms and Languages: Dutch Influencers and Legal Disclosures on Instagram YouTube and TikTok
[NLP-26] 跨平台和语言：Instagram YouTube和TikTok上的荷兰影响者和法律披露

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12451
作者: Haoyang Gui,Thales Bertaglia,Catalina Goanta,Sybe de Vries,Gerasimos Spanakis
关键词: social media fuels, growing influencer economy, social media, fuels a growing, monetization on social
中文关键词: 社交媒体推动了不断增长的影响者经济、社交媒体推动了日益增长的社交货币化
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Social and Information Networks (cs.SI)
备注: Accept for publication at the 16th International Conference on Advances in Social Networks Analysis and Mining -ASONAM-2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Content monetization on social media fuels a growing influencer economy. Influencer marketing remains largely undisclosed or inappropriately disclosed on social media. Non-disclosure issues have become a priority for national and supranational authorities worldwide, who are starting to impose increasingly harsher sanctions on them. This paper proposes a transparent methodology for measuring whether and how influencers comply with disclosures based on legal standards. We introduce a novel distinction between disclosures that are legally sufficient (green) and legally insufficient (yellow). We apply this methodology to an original dataset reflecting the content of 150 Dutch influencers publicly registered with the Dutch Media Authority based on recently introduced registration obligations. The dataset consists of 292,315 posts and is multi-language (English and Dutch) and cross-platform (Instagram, YouTube and TikTok). We find that influencer marketing remains generally underdisclosed on social media, and that bigger influencers are not necessarily more compliant with disclosure standards.
摘要：社交媒体上的内容货币化推动了越来越有影响力的经济。在社交媒体上，有影响力的营销在很大程度上仍然没有披露或不适当地披露。保密问题已成为世界各地国家和超国家当局的优先事项，它们正开始对它们实施越来越严厉的制裁。本文提出了一种透明的方法来衡量有影响力的人是否以及如何遵守基于法律标准的披露。我们在法律上充分的披露(绿色)和法律不足的披露(黄色)之间引入了一种新的区别。我们将这种方法应用于反映150名荷兰有影响力的人的原始数据集，这些人根据最近引入的注册义务在荷兰媒体管理局公开注册。该数据集包括292315个帖子，有多种语言(英语和荷兰语)和跨平台(Instagram、YouTube和TikTok)。我们发现，有影响力的营销在社交媒体上总体上仍然披露不足，更有影响力的人不一定更符合披露标准。

[NLP-27] Sharif-STR at SemEval-2024 Task 1: Transformer as a Regression Model for Fine-Grained Scoring of Textual Semantic Relations
[NLP-27] Sharif-TR参加SemEval-2024任务1：Transformer作为文本语义关系细粒度评分的回归模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12426
作者: Seyedeh Fatemeh Ebrahimi,Karim Akhavan Azari,Amirmasoud Iravani,Hadi Alizadeh,Zeinab Sadat Taghavi,Hossein Sameti
关键词: Semantic Textual Relatedness, Textual Relatedness holds, Natural Language Processing, Relatedness holds significant, Semantic Textual
中文关键词: 语义文本相关性，文本相关性成立，自然语言处理，相关性成立，语义文本
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 10 pages, 9 figures, 4 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Semantic Textual Relatedness holds significant relevance in Natural Language Processing, finding applications across various domains. Traditionally, approaches to STR have relied on knowledge-based and statistical methods. However, with the emergence of Large Language Models, there has been a paradigm shift, ushering in new methodologies. In this paper, we delve into the investigation of sentence-level STR within Track A (Supervised) by leveraging fine-tuning techniques on the RoBERTa transformer. Our study focuses on assessing the efficacy of this approach across different languages. Notably, our findings indicate promising advancements in STR performance, particularly in Latin languages. Specifically, our results demonstrate notable improvements in English, achieving a correlation of 0.82 and securing a commendable 19th rank. Similarly, in Spanish, we achieved a correlation of 0.67, securing the 15th position. However, our approach encounters challenges in languages like Arabic, where we observed a correlation of only 0.38, resulting in a 20th rank.
摘要：语义文本关联性在自然语言处理中具有重要的相关性，在各个领域都有应用。传统上，科学研究的方法依赖于基于知识的方法和统计方法。然而，随着大型语言模型的出现，出现了范式转变，带来了新的方法。在本文中，我们利用Roberta转换器上的微调技术，深入研究了音轨A(有监督)内的句子级STR。我们的研究重点是评估这种方法在不同语言中的有效性。值得注意的是，我们的发现表明，STR的表现有了很大的进步，特别是在拉丁语方面。具体地说，我们的结果显示出显著的英语进步，达到了0.82的相关性，并获得了值得称赞的第19位。同样，在西班牙语中，我们的相关度为0.67，确保了第15名的位置。然而，我们的方法在阿拉伯语等语言中遇到了挑战，我们发现相关性只有0.38，排在第20位。

[NLP-28] Navigating the Noisy Crowd: Finding Key Information for Claim Verification
[NLP-28] 引导喧闹人群：寻找索赔验证的关键信息

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12425
作者: Haisong Gong,Huanhuan Ma,Qiang Liu,Shu Wu,Liang Wang
关键词: Claim, Claim verification, evidence, task that involves, involves assessing
中文关键词: 索赔、索赔验证、证据、涉及、涉及评估的任务
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Claim verification is a task that involves assessing the truthfulness of a given claim based on multiple evidence pieces. Using large language models (LLMs) for claim verification is a promising way. However, simply feeding all the evidence pieces to an LLM and asking if the claim is factual does not yield good results. The challenge lies in the noisy nature of both the evidence and the claim: evidence passages typically contain irrelevant information, with the key facts hidden within the context, while claims often convey multiple aspects simultaneously. To navigate this “noisy crowd” of information, we propose EACon (Evidence Abstraction and Claim Deconstruction), a framework designed to find key information within evidence and verify each aspect of a claim separately. EACon first finds keywords from the claim and employs fuzzy matching to select relevant keywords for each raw evidence piece. These keywords serve as a guide to extract and summarize critical information into abstracted evidence. Subsequently, EACon deconstructs the original claim into subclaims, which are then verified against both abstracted and raw evidence individually. We evaluate EACon using two open-source LLMs on two challenging datasets. Results demonstrate that EACon consistently and substantially improve LLMs’ performance in claim verification.
摘要：索赔核实是一项基于多个证据对给定索赔的真实性进行评估的任务。使用大型语言模型(LLM)进行索赔验证是一种很有前途的方法。然而，简单地将所有证据提供给LLM并询问声明是否属实并不会产生好的结果。挑战在于证据和主张的嘈杂性质：证据段落通常包含无关的信息，关键事实隐藏在上下文中，而主张往往同时传达多个方面。为了驾驭这个“嘈杂的人群”的信息，我们提出了EACon(证据抽象和索赔解构)，这是一个框架，旨在找到证据中的关键信息，并分别验证索赔的每个方面。EACon首先从索赔中找到关键字，并使用模糊匹配为每个原始证据选择相关关键字。这些关键字可作为提取关键信息并将其汇总为抽象证据的指南。随后，EACon将原始索赔解构为子索赔，然后分别对照抽象证据和原始证据进行核实。我们使用两个开源LLMS在两个具有挑战性的数据集上对EACon进行了评估。结果表明，EACon持续显著地提高了LLMS在索赔验证中的性能。

[NLP-29] urkishMMLU: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding in Turkish
[NLP-29] urkishMMLU：用土耳其语衡量大规模多任务语言理解

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12402
作者: Arda Yüksel,Abdullatif Köksal,Lütfi Kerem Şenel,Anna Korhonen,Hinrich Schütze
关键词: Multiple choice question, Large Language Models, Multiple choice, abilities of Large, Large Language
中文关键词: 多项选择题，大型语言模型，多项选择，大型语言的能力
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Multiple choice question answering tasks evaluate the reasoning, comprehension, and mathematical abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). While existing benchmarks employ automatic translation for multilingual evaluation, this approach is error-prone and potentially introduces culturally biased questions, especially in social sciences. We introduce the first multitask, multiple-choice Turkish QA benchmark, TurkishMMLU, to evaluate LLMs’ understanding of the Turkish language. TurkishMMLU includes over 10,000 questions, covering 9 different subjects from Turkish high-school education curricula. These questions are written by curriculum experts, suitable for the high-school curricula in Turkey, covering subjects ranging from natural sciences and math questions to more culturally representative topics such as Turkish Literature and the history of the Turkish Republic. We evaluate over 20 LLMs, including multilingual open-source (e.g., Gemma, Llama, MT5), closed-source (GPT 4o, Claude, Gemini), and Turkish-adapted (e.g., Trendyol) models. We provide an extensive evaluation, including zero-shot and few-shot evaluation of LLMs, chain-of-thought reasoning, and question difficulty analysis along with model performance. We provide an in-depth analysis of the Turkish capabilities and limitations of current LLMs to provide insights for future LLMs for the Turkish language. We publicly release our code for the dataset and evaluation: this https URL.
摘要：多项选择题测试了大型语言模型的推理、理解和数学能力。虽然现有的基准使用自动翻译进行多语言评估，但这种方法容易出错，并可能引入带有文化偏见的问题，特别是在社会科学领域。我们引入了第一个多任务、多选择的土耳其语QA基准，TurishMMLU来评估LLMS对土耳其语的理解。土耳其MMLU包括10,000多个问题，涵盖土耳其高中教育课程中的9个不同科目。这些问题由课程专家撰写，适合土耳其的高中课程，涵盖从自然科学和数学问题到更具文化代表性的主题，如土耳其文学和土耳其共和国历史。我们评估了20多个LLMS，包括多语言开源(例如Gema、Llama、MT5)、封闭源代码(GPT 40、Claude、Gemini)和土耳其语适配模型(例如Trendyol)。我们提供了广泛的评估，包括对LLMS的零点和少点评估，思维链推理，问题难度分析以及模型性能。我们深入分析了当前土耳其语的能力和局限性，为未来的土耳其语LLM提供了洞察力。我们公开发布我们的数据集和评估代码：这个HTTPS URL。

[NLP-30] PersLLM: A Personified Training Approach for Large Language Models
[NLP-30] CLARLLM：大型语言模型的个性化训练方法

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12393
作者: Zheni Zeng,Jiayi Chen,Huimin Chen,Yukun Yan,Yuxuan Chen,Zhiyuan Liu,Maosong Sun
关键词: Large language models, Large language, language models exhibit, models exhibit aspects, exhibit aspects
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，大型语言，语言模型展示，模型展示方面，展示方面
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computers and Society (cs.CY)
备注: 10 pages for main text, 5 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models exhibit aspects of human-level intelligence that catalyze their application as human-like agents in domains such as social simulations, human-machine interactions, and collaborative multi-agent systems. However, the absence of distinct personalities, such as displaying ingratiating behaviors, inconsistent opinions, and uniform response patterns, diminish LLMs utility in practical applications. Addressing this, the development of personality traits in LLMs emerges as a crucial area of research to unlock their latent potential. Existing methods to personify LLMs generally involve strategies like employing stylized training data for instruction tuning or using prompt engineering to simulate different personalities. These methods only capture superficial linguistic styles instead of the core of personalities and are therefore not stable. In this study, we propose PersLLM, integrating psychology-grounded principles of personality: social practice, consistency, and dynamic development, into a comprehensive training methodology. We incorporate personality traits directly into the model parameters, enhancing the model’s resistance to induction, promoting consistency, and supporting the dynamic evolution of personality. Single-agent evaluation validates our method’s superiority, as it produces responses more aligned with reference personalities compared to other approaches. Case studies for multi-agent communication highlight its benefits in enhancing opinion consistency within individual agents and fostering collaborative creativity among multiple agents in dialogue contexts, potentially benefiting human simulation and multi-agent cooperation. Additionally, human-agent interaction evaluations indicate that our personified models significantly enhance interactive experiences, underscoring the practical implications of our research.
摘要：大型语言模型展示了人类智能的各个方面，这些智能催化它们作为类似人类的代理应用于社会模拟、人机交互和协作多代理系统等领域。然而，缺乏明显的个性特征，如表现出讨好的行为、不一致的意见和统一的反应模式，降低了LLMS在实际应用中的实用性。为了解决这一问题，LLMS的人格特征发展成为释放其潜在潜力的关键研究领域。现有的个性化LLM的方法通常包括使用风格化的训练数据进行教学调整或使用提示工程来模拟不同的个性等策略。这些方法只捕捉表面的语言风格，而不是个性的核心，因此不稳定。在这项研究中，我们提出了PersLLM，将基于心理学的人格原则：社会实践、一致性和动态发展，整合到一个全面的训练方法中。我们将人格特征直接纳入到模型参数中，增强了模型对归纳的抵抗力，促进了一致性，并支持人格的动态演变。单代理评估验证了我们方法的优越性，因为与其他方法相比，它产生的反应更符合参考个性。多智能体通信的案例研究突出了其在提高个体智能体内部的意见一致性和培养对话环境中多智能体之间的协作创造力方面的好处，潜在地有利于人类模拟和多智能体合作。此外，人-代理交互评估表明，我们的人格化模型显著增强了交互体验，强调了我们研究的实际意义。

[NLP-31] Morphosyntactic Analysis for CHILDES
[NLP-31] 儿童形态语法分析

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12389
作者: Houjun Liu,Brian MacWhinney
关键词: Language development researchers, development researchers, researchers are interested, interested in comparing, comparing the process
中文关键词: 语言开发研究人员，开发研究人员，研究人员有兴趣，有兴趣比较，比较过程
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Language development researchers are interested in comparing the process of language learning across languages. Unfortunately, it has been difficult to construct a consistent quantitative framework for such comparisons. However, recent advances in AI (Artificial Intelligence) and ML (Machine Learning) are providing new methods for ASR (automatic speech recognition) and NLP (natural language processing) that can be brought to bear on this problem. Using the Batchalign2 program (Liu et al., 2023), we have been transcribing and linking data for the CHILDES database and have applied the UD (Universal Dependencies) framework to provide a consistent and comparable morphosyntactic analysis for 27 languages. These new resources open possibilities for deeper crosslinguistic study of language learning.
摘要：语言发展研究人员有兴趣比较跨语言的语言学习过程。不幸的是，很难为此类比较构建一致的量化框架。然而，人工智能（人工智能）和ML（机器学习）的最新进展为ASB（自动语音识别）和NLP（自然语言处理）提供了可以解决这一问题的新方法。使用Batchspel 2程序（Liu等人，2023年），我们一直在转录和链接CHILDES数据库的数据，并应用UD（通用附属）框架为27种语言提供一致且可比的形态语法分析。这些新资源为语言学习的更深入的跨语言研究开辟了可能性。

[NLP-32] Deep Learning-based Sentiment Analysis of Olympics Tweets
[NLP-32] 基于深度学习的奥运推文情绪分析

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12376
作者: Indranil Bandyopadhyay,Rahul Karmakar
关键词: natural language processing, text emotional tone, analyzing subjective information, language processing, specific topics
中文关键词: 自然语言处理、文本情感语气、分析主观信息、语言处理、特定主题
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Sentiment analysis (SA), is an approach of natural language processing (NLP) for determining a text’s emotional tone by analyzing subjective information such as views, feelings, and attitudes toward specific topics, products, services, events, or experiences. This study attempts to develop an advanced deep learning (DL) model for SA to understand global audience emotions through tweets in the context of the Olympic Games. The findings represent global attitudes around the Olympics and contribute to advancing the SA models. We have used NLP for tweet pre-processing and sophisticated DL models for arguing with SA, this research enhances the reliability and accuracy of sentiment classification. The study focuses on data selection, preprocessing, visualization, feature extraction, and model building, featuring a baseline Naïve Bayes (NB) model and three advanced DL models: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), and Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT). The results of the experiments show that the BERT model can efficiently classify sentiments related to the Olympics, achieving the highest accuracy of 99.23%.
摘要：情感分析(SA)是一种自然语言处理(NLP)的方法，通过分析主观信息，如对特定主题、产品、服务、事件或体验的看法、感觉和态度，确定文本的情感基调。本研究试图为SA开发一个先进的深度学习模型，以通过奥运会背景下的推文来理解全球观众的情绪。这些发现代表了全球对奥运会的态度，并有助于推动SA模式的发展。我们使用NLP对推文进行了预处理，并将复杂的DL模型用于与SA的争论，提高了情感分类的可靠性和准确性。这项研究的重点是数据选择、预处理、可视化、特征提取和模型建立，包括一个基线朴素贝叶斯(NB)模型和三个先进的DL模型：卷积神经网络(CNN)、双向长期短期记忆(BiLSTM)和来自Transformers的双向编码器表示(BERT)。实验结果表明，BERT模型能够有效地对奥运会相关情感进行分类，最高准确率达到99.23%。

[NLP-33] NavGPT-2: Unleashing Navigational Reasoning Capability for Large Vision-Language Models
[NLP-33] NavGPT-2：释放大型视觉语言模型的导航推理能力

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12366
作者: Gengze Zhou,Yicong Hong,Zun Wang,Xin Eric Wang,Qi Wu
关键词: Large Language Models, advancements in Large, Large Language, remarkable advancements, burgeoning initiative
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、大型、大型语言的进步、显着的进步、蓬勃发展的倡议
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Robotics (cs.RO)
备注: Accepted to ECCV 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Capitalizing on the remarkable advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs), there is a burgeoning initiative to harness LLMs for instruction following robotic navigation. Such a trend underscores the potential of LLMs to generalize navigational reasoning and diverse language understanding. However, a significant discrepancy in agent performance is observed when integrating LLMs in the Vision-and-Language navigation (VLN) tasks compared to previous downstream specialist models. Furthermore, the inherent capacity of language to interpret and facilitate communication in agent interactions is often underutilized in these integrations. In this work, we strive to bridge the divide between VLN-specialized models and LLM-based navigation paradigms, while maintaining the interpretative prowess of LLMs in generating linguistic navigational reasoning. By aligning visual content in a frozen LLM, we encompass visual observation comprehension for LLMs and exploit a way to incorporate LLMs and navigation policy networks for effective action predictions and navigational reasoning. We demonstrate the data efficiency of the proposed methods and eliminate the gap between LM-based agents and state-of-the-art VLN specialists.
摘要：利用大型语言模型(LLM)的显著进步，出现了一项新兴的倡议，即利用LLM进行机器人导航后的教学。这一趋势突显了LLMS推广导航推理和多样化语言理解的潜力。然而，与以前的下游专家模型相比，在视觉与语言导航(VLN)任务中集成LLM时，观察到代理性能存在显著差异。此外，语言在代理交互中解释和促进交流的固有能力在这些集成中往往没有得到充分利用。在这项工作中，我们努力弥合VLN专用模型和基于LLM的导航范例之间的分歧，同时保持LLMS在生成语言导航推理方面的解释能力。通过在冻结的LLM中对齐视觉内容，我们包含了对LLM的视觉观察理解，并开发了一种将LLM和导航策略网络结合起来的方法，以实现有效的行动预测和导航推理。我们证明了提出的方法的数据效率，并消除了基于LM的代理和最先进的VLN专家之间的差距。

[NLP-34] Conversational Query Reformulation with the Guidance of Retrieved Documents
[NLP-34] 以检索文档为指导的对话式查询重构

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12363
作者: Jeonghyun Park,Hwanhee Lee
关键词: retrieve relevant passages, Conversational search seeks, Conversational Query Reformulation, seeks to retrieve, retrieve relevant
中文关键词: 检索相关段落，对话式搜索寻求，对话式查询重组，寻求检索，检索相关
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 8 pages, 5 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Conversational search seeks to retrieve relevant passages for the given questions in Conversational QA (ConvQA). Questions in ConvQA face challenges such as omissions and coreferences, making it difficult to obtain desired search results. Conversational Query Reformulation (CQR) transforms these current queries into de-contextualized forms to resolve these issues. However, existing CQR methods focus on rewriting human-friendly queries, which may not always yield optimal search results for the retriever. To overcome this challenge, we introduce GuideCQR, a framework that utilizes guided documents to refine queries, ensuring that they are optimal for retrievers. Specifically, we augment keywords, generate expected answers from the re-ranked documents, and unify them with the filtering process. Experimental results show that queries enhanced by guided documents outperform previous CQR methods. Especially, GuideCQR surpasses the performance of Large Language Model (LLM) prompt-powered approaches and demonstrates the importance of the guided documents in formulating retriever-friendly queries across diverse setups.
摘要：会话搜索旨在检索会话问答(ConvQA)中给定问题的相关段落。ConvQA中的问题面临着遗漏和引用等挑战，使得获得所需的搜索结果变得困难。会话式查询重构将当前查询转换为去上下文的形式来解决这些问题。然而，现有的CQR方法侧重于重写人类友好的查询，这可能并不总是为检索者产生最优的搜索结果。为了克服这一挑战，我们引入了GuideCQR，这是一个利用指导文档来优化查询的框架，确保它们是最适合检索者的。具体地说，我们增加关键字，从重新排序的文档生成预期的答案，并将它们与过滤过程统一起来。实验结果表明，由引导文档增强的查询性能优于以往的CQR方法。特别是，GuideCQR的性能超过了大型语言模型(LLM)提示驱动的方法，并证明了引导文档在制定跨不同设置的检索器友好查询方面的重要性。

[NLP-35] ProcTag: Process Tagging for Assessing the Efficacy of Document Instruction Data
[NLP-35] ProcTAG：用于评估文档指令数据有效性的流程标签

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12358
作者: Yufan Shen,Chuwei Luo,Zhaoqing Zhu,Yang Chen,Qi Zheng,Zhi Yu,Jiajun Bu,Cong Yao
关键词: large language models, multimodal large language, language models, large language, visual question answering
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、多模式大型语言、语言模型、大型语言、视觉问答
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recently, large language models (LLMs) and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have demonstrated promising results on document visual question answering (VQA) task, particularly after training on document instruction datasets. An effective evaluation method for document instruction data is crucial in constructing instruction data with high efficacy, which, in turn, facilitates the training of LLMs and MLLMs for document VQA. However, most existing evaluation methods for instruction data are limited to the textual content of the instructions themselves, thereby hindering the effective assessment of document instruction datasets and constraining their construction. In this paper, we propose ProcTag, a data-oriented method that assesses the efficacy of document instruction data. ProcTag innovatively performs tagging on the execution process of instructions rather than the instruction text itself. By leveraging the diversity and complexity of these tags to assess the efficacy of the given dataset, ProcTag enables selective sampling or filtering of document instructions. Furthermore, DocLayPrompt, a novel semi-structured layout-aware document prompting strategy, is proposed for effectively representing documents. Experiments demonstrate that sampling existing open-sourced and generated document VQA/instruction datasets with ProcTag significantly outperforms current methods for evaluating instruction data. Impressively, with ProcTag-based sampling in the generated document datasets, only 30.5% of the document instructions are required to achieve 100% efficacy compared to the complete dataset. The code is publicly available at this https URL.
摘要：最近，大语言模型和多通道大语言模型在文档视觉问答任务中显示出了良好的效果，特别是在文档教学数据集上进行了训练之后。有效的文档教学数据评估方法是构建高效教学数据的关键，而高效的教学数据又有助于培养文档VQA的LLMS和MLLMS。然而，现有的教学数据评价方法大多局限于指令本身的文本内容，从而阻碍了对文档教学数据集的有效评价，制约了文档教学数据集的构建。在本文中，我们提出了一种面向数据的方法ProcTag，用于评估文档说明数据的有效性。ProcTag创新性地对指令的执行过程而不是指令文本本身执行标记。通过利用这些标签的多样性和复杂性来评估给定数据集的有效性，ProcTag可以对文档说明进行选择性采样或过滤。在此基础上，提出了一种新的半结构化布局感知文档提示策略DocLayPrompt，用于有效地表示文档。实验表明，使用ProcTag对现有的开源和生成的文档VQA/指令数据集进行采样的性能明显优于现有的指令数据评估方法。令人印象深刻的是，在生成的文档数据集中使用基于ProcTag的采样，与完整的数据集相比，只需要30.5%的文档指令就可以达到100%的效果。代码可在此HTTPS URL上公开获得。

[NLP-36] he Better Angels of Machine Personality: How Personality Relates to LLM Safety
[NLP-36] 机器人格的更好天使：人格如何与LLM安全相关

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12344
作者: Jie Zhang,Dongrui Liu,Chen Qian,Ziyue Gan,Yong Liu,Yu Qiao,Jing Shao
关键词: Large Language Models, personality traits, Personality, human society, Language Models
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、性格特征、性格、人类社会、语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computers and Society (cs.CY)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Personality psychologists have analyzed the relationship between personality and safety behaviors in human society. Although Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate personality traits, the relationship between personality traits and safety abilities in LLMs still remains a mystery. In this paper, we discover that LLMs’ personality traits are closely related to their safety abilities, i.e., toxicity, privacy, and fairness, based on the reliable MBTI-M scale. Meanwhile, the safety alignment generally increases various LLMs’ Extraversion, Sensing, and Judging traits. According to such findings, we can edit LLMs’ personality traits and improve their safety performance, e.g., inducing personality from ISTJ to ISTP resulted in a relative improvement of approximately 43% and 10% in privacy and fairness performance, respectively. Additionally, we find that LLMs with different personality traits are differentially susceptible to jailbreak. This study pioneers the investigation of LLM safety from a personality perspective, providing new insights into LLM safety enhancement.
摘要：人格心理学家分析了人类社会中人格与安全行为的关系。虽然大型语言模型(LLM)显示了人格特征，但LLM的人格特征和安全能力之间的关系仍然是一个谜。本文基于可靠的MBTI-M量表，发现低收入人群的人格特征与其安全能力密切相关，即毒性、隐私性和公平性。同时，安全对齐总体上增加了不同LLMS的外倾性、感知性和判断性。根据这些发现，我们可以编辑LLMS的人格特征并提高其安全性能，例如，从ISTJ到ISTP诱导个性导致隐私和公平性能分别相对提高约43%和10%。此外，我们发现具有不同人格特征的低层管理人员对越狱的易感性不同。这项研究开创了从人格角度研究激光飞行安全性的先河，为激光飞行安全性的提高提供了新的视角。

[NLP-37] Word Embedding Dimension Reduction via Weakly-Supervised Feature Selection
[NLP-37] 通过弱监督特征选择的词嵌入降维

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12342
作者: Jintang Xue,Yun-Cheng Wang,Chengwei Wei,C.-C. Jay Kuo
关键词: natural language processing, word embedding converts, natural language, vector space, word embedding
中文关键词: 自然语言处理、词嵌入转换、自然语言、载体空间、词嵌入
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As a fundamental task in natural language processing, word embedding converts each word into a representation in a vector space. A challenge with word embedding is that as the vocabulary grows, the vector space’s dimension increases and it can lead to a vast model size. Storing and processing word vectors are resource-demanding, especially for mobile edge-devices applications. This paper explores word embedding dimension reduction. To balance computational costs and performance, we propose an efficient and effective weakly-supervised feature selection method, named WordFS. It has two variants, each utilizing novel criteria for feature selection. Experiments conducted on various tasks (e.g., word and sentence similarity and binary and multi-class classification) indicate that the proposed WordFS model outperforms other dimension reduction methods at lower computational costs.
