摘要：作为自然语言处理中的一项基本任务，单词嵌入将每个单词转换为载体空间中的表示。单词嵌入的一个挑战是，随着词汇量的增长，载体空间的维度也会增加，并且可能会导致模型大小巨大。存储和处理字载体对资源要求很高，尤其是对于移动边缘设备应用程序。本文探讨了词嵌入降维。为了平衡计算成本和性能，我们提出了一种高效且有效的弱监督特征选择方法，名为WordFS。它有两个变体，每个变体都利用新颖的特征选择标准。对各种任务进行的实验（例如，单词和句子相似性以及二进制和多类分类）表明，提出的WordFS模型以更低的计算成本优于其他降维方法。

[NLP-38] M2DS: Multilingual Dataset for Multi-document Summarisation
[NLP-38] M2 DS：用于多文档摘要的多语言数据集

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12336
作者: Kushan Hewapathirana(1 and 2),Nisansa de Silva(1),C.D. Athuraliya(2) ((1) Department of Computer Science amp; Engineering, University of Moratuwa, Sri Lanka, (2) ConscientAI, Sri Lanka)
关键词: distil key insights, evolving digital era, rapidly evolving digital, Multi-document Summarisation, rapidly evolving
中文关键词: 关键见解、不断发展的数字时代、快速发展的数字、多文档总结、快速发展
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In the rapidly evolving digital era, there is an increasing demand for concise information as individuals seek to distil key insights from various sources. Recent attention from researchers on Multi-document Summarisation (MDS) has resulted in diverse datasets covering customer reviews, academic papers, medical and legal documents, and news articles. However, the English-centric nature of these datasets has created a conspicuous void for multilingual datasets in today’s globalised digital landscape, where linguistic diversity is celebrated. Media platforms such as British Broadcasting Corporation (BBC) have disseminated news in 20+ languages for decades. With only 380 million people speaking English natively as their first language, accounting for less than 5% of the global population, the vast majority primarily relies on other languages. These facts underscore the need for inclusivity in MDS research, utilising resources from diverse languages. Recognising this gap, we present the Multilingual Dataset for Multi-document Summarisation (M2DS), which, to the best of our knowledge, is the first dataset of its kind. It includes document-summary pairs in five languages from BBC articles published during the 2010-2023 period. This paper introduces M2DS, emphasising its unique multilingual aspect, and includes baseline scores from state-of-the-art MDS models evaluated on our dataset.
摘要：在快速发展的数字时代，随着个人寻求从各种来源提取关键见解，对简明信息的需求越来越大。最近，研究人员对多文档摘要(MDS)的关注导致了涵盖客户评论、学术论文、医疗和法律文档以及新闻文章的各种数据集。然而，这些数据集以英语为中心的本质，在当今提倡语言多样性的全球化数字版图中，为多语言数据集创造了一个明显的空白。几十年来，英国广播公司(BBC)等媒体平台已经用20多种语言传播新闻。由于只有3.8亿人将英语作为第一语言，占全球人口的不到5%，绝大多数人主要依赖其他语言。这些事实突显了在MDS研究中需要包容性，利用不同语言的资源。认识到这一差距，我们提出了用于多文档摘要的多语言数据集(M2DS)，据我们所知，这是此类数据集中的第一个。它包括2010-2023年期间发表的BBC文章中五种语言的文件摘要对。本文介绍了M2DS，强调其独特的多语言方面，并包括在我们的数据集上评估的最先进的MDS模型的基线分数。

[NLP-39] MEDFuse: Multimodal EHR Data Fusion with Masked Lab-Test Modeling and Large Language Models
[NLP-39] MEDFuse：具有掩蔽实验室测试建模和大型语言模型的多模式EHR数据融合

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12309
作者: Thao Minh Nguyen Phan,Cong-Tinh Dao,Chenwei Wu,Jian-Zhe Wang,Shun Liu,Jun-En Ding,David Restrepo,Feng Liu,Fang-Ming Hung,Wen-Chih Peng
关键词: Electronic health records, multimodal EHR data, Electronic health, EHR Data Fusion, multimodal EHR
中文关键词: 电子健康记录、多模式EHR数据、电子健康、EHR数据融合、多模式EHR
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Electronic health records (EHRs) are multimodal by nature, consisting of structured tabular features like lab tests and unstructured clinical notes. In real-life clinical practice, doctors use complementary multimodal EHR data sources to get a clearer picture of patients’ health and support clinical decision-making. However, most EHR predictive models do not reflect these procedures, as they either focus on a single modality or overlook the inter-modality interactions/redundancy. In this work, we propose MEDFuse, a Multimodal EHR Data Fusion framework that incorporates masked lab-test modeling and large language models (LLMs) to effectively integrate structured and unstructured medical data. MEDFuse leverages multimodal embeddings extracted from two sources: LLMs fine-tuned on free clinical text and masked tabular transformers trained on structured lab test results. We design a disentangled transformer module, optimized by a mutual information loss to 1) decouple modality-specific and modality-shared information and 2) extract useful joint representation from the noise and redundancy present in clinical notes. Through comprehensive validation on the public MIMIC-III dataset and the in-house FEMH dataset, MEDFuse demonstrates great potential in advancing clinical predictions, achieving over 90% F1 score in the 10-disease multi-label classification task.
摘要：电子健康记录(EHR)本质上是多模式的，由实验室测试和非结构化临床记录等结构化表格特征组成。在现实的临床实践中，医生使用互补的多模式EHR数据源，以更清楚地了解患者的健康状况，并支持临床决策。然而，大多数EHR预测模型没有反映这些过程，因为它们要么专注于单一的通道，要么忽略了通道间的交互/冗余。在这项工作中，我们提出了MEDFuse，一个多模式的EHR数据融合框架，它结合了掩蔽的实验室测试建模和大型语言模型(LLMS)来有效地集成结构化和非结构化的医疗数据。MEDFuse利用从两个来源提取的多模式嵌入：根据免费临床文本微调的LLM和根据结构化实验室测试结果培训的屏蔽表格变压器。我们设计了一个分离的转换器模块，通过互信息损失进行优化，以1)分离特定于通道和通道共享的信息，以及2)从临床记录中存在的噪声和冗余中提取有用的关节表示。通过对公共MIMIC-III数据集和内部FEMH数据集的全面验证，MEDFuse在推进临床预测方面显示出巨大的潜力，在10种疾病的多标签分类任务中达到了90%以上的F1分数。

[NLP-40] Multimodal Reranking for Knowledge-Intensive Visual Question Answering
[NLP-40] 知识密集型视觉问答的多模式重新排序

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12277
作者: Haoyang Wen,Honglei Zhuang,Hamed Zamani,Alexander Hauptmann,Michael Bendersky
关键词: Knowledge-intensive visual question, Knowledge-intensive visual, answering requires models, visual question answering, question answering requires
中文关键词: 知识密集型视觉问题，知识密集型视觉，回答需要模型，视觉问题回答，问题回答需要
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Knowledge-intensive visual question answering requires models to effectively use external knowledge to help answer visual questions. A typical pipeline includes a knowledge retriever and an answer generator. However, a retriever that utilizes local information, such as an image patch, may not provide reliable question-candidate relevance scores. Besides, the two-tower architecture also limits the relevance score modeling of a retriever to select top candidates for answer generator reasoning. In this paper, we introduce an additional module, a multi-modal reranker, to improve the ranking quality of knowledge candidates for answer generation. Our reranking module takes multi-modal information from both candidates and questions and performs cross-item interaction for better relevance score modeling. Experiments on OK-VQA and A-OKVQA show that multi-modal reranker from distant supervision provides consistent improvements. We also find a training-testing discrepancy with reranking in answer generation, where performance improves if training knowledge candidates are similar to or noisier than those used in testing.
摘要：知识密集型视觉问答要求模型有效地使用外部知识来帮助回答视觉问题。典型的管道包括知识检索器和答案生成器。然而，使用本地信息(例如图像补丁)的检索器可能不会提供可靠的候选问题相关性分数。此外，两塔结构还限制了检索者为答案生成器推理选择最佳候选者的相关性得分建模。本文引入了一个附加模块–多模式重排器，以提高答案生成中知识候选的排序质量。我们的重新排序模块从候选人和问题中获取多模式信息，并执行跨项目交互，以更好地进行相关性得分建模。在OK-VQA和A-OKVQA上的实验表明，来自远程监控的多模式重定器提供了一致的改进。我们还发现，在答案生成中，训练测试与重新排序存在差异，如果训练知识候选者与测试中使用的训练知识相似或噪声更大，则性能会提高。

[NLP-41] In-Context Probing Approximates Influence Function for Data Valuation
[NLP-41] 上下文探索逼近数据估值的影响函数

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12259
作者: Cathy Jiao,Gary Gao,Chenyan Xiong
关键词: curating high-quality datasets, train large language, large language models, Data valuation quantifies, training data
中文关键词: 策划高质量数据集、训练大型语言、大型语言模型、数据估值量化、训练数据
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Data valuation quantifies the value of training data, and is used for data attribution (i.e., determining the contribution of training data towards model predictions), and data selection; both of which are important for curating high-quality datasets to train large language models. In our paper, we show that data valuation through in-context probing (i.e., prompting a LLM) approximates influence functions for selecting training data. We provide a theoretical sketch on this connection based on transformer models performing “implicit” gradient descent on its in-context inputs. Our empirical findings show that in-context probing and gradient-based influence frameworks are similar in how they rank training data. Furthermore, fine-tuning experiments on data selected by either method reveal similar model performance.
摘要：数据估值量化训练数据的价值，用于数据属性（即确定训练数据对模型预测的贡献）以及数据选择;这两者对于策划高质量数据集以训练大型语言模型都很重要。在我们的论文中，我们展示了通过上下文探测（即，提示LLM）逼近用于选择训练数据的影响函数。我们基于对其上下文输入执行“隐式”梯度下降的Transformer模型，提供了关于这种连接的理论草图。我们的经验研究结果表明，上下文探测和基于梯度的影响框架在对训练数据进行排名方面是相似的。此外，对通过两种方法选择的数据进行微调实验都显示出相似的模型性能。

[NLP-42] Lacuna Language Learning: Leveraging RNNs for Ranked Text Completion in Digitized Coptic Manuscripts
[NLP-42] Lacuna语言学习：利用RNN在数字化科普特手稿中进行排名文本完成

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12247
作者: Lauren Levine,Cindy Tung Li,Lydia Bremer-McCollum,Nicholas Wagner,Amir Zeldes
关键词: Ancient manuscripts, frequently damaged, RNN model, Ancient, RNN
中文关键词: 古代手稿，经常损坏，RNN模型，古代，RNN
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Machine Learning for Ancient Languages, ACL 2024 Workshop, 15 August 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Ancient manuscripts are frequently damaged, containing gaps in the text known as lacunae. In this paper, we present a bidirectional RNN model for character prediction of Coptic characters in manuscript lacunae. Our best model performs with 72% accuracy on single character reconstruction, but falls to 37% when reconstructing lacunae of various lengths. While not suitable for definitive manuscript reconstruction, we argue that our RNN model can help scholars rank the likelihood of textual reconstructions. As evidence, we use our RNN model to rank reconstructions in two early Coptic manuscripts. Our investigation shows that neural models can augment traditional methods of textual restoration, providing scholars with an additional tool to assess lacunae in Coptic manuscripts.
摘要：古代手稿经常被损坏，文本中存在被称为空白的空白。在本文中，我们提出了一种双向RNN模型，用于手稿空白中科普特字符的字符预测。我们的最佳模型在单个字符重建中的准确率为72%，但在重建各种长度的空位时，准确率降至37%。虽然不适合最终的手稿重建，但我们认为我们的RNN模型可以帮助学者对文本重建的可能性进行排名。作为证据，我们使用RNN模型对两份早期科普特手稿中的重建进行排名。我们的调查表明，神经模型可以增强传统的文本恢复方法，为学者提供了评估科普特手稿中缺陷的额外工具。

[NLP-43] Questionable practices in machine learning
[NLP-43] 机器学习中可疑的实践

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12220
作者: Gavin Leech,Juan J. Vazquez,Misha Yagudin,Niclas Kupper,Laurence Aitchison
关键词: Evaluating modern, Evaluating, Abstract, practices, research
中文关键词: 评估现代、评估、抽象、实践、研究
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computers and Society (cs.CY)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Evaluating modern ML models is hard. The strong incentive for researchers and companies to report a state-of-the-art result on some metric often leads to questionable research practices (QRPs): bad practices which fall short of outright research fraud. We describe 43 such practices which can undermine reported results, giving examples where possible. Our list emphasises the evaluation of large language models (LLMs) on public benchmarks. We also discuss “irreproducible research practices”, i.e. decisions that make it difficult or impossible for other researchers to reproduce, build on or audit previous research.
摘要：评估现代ML模型很困难。研究人员和公司报告某些指标的最新结果的强烈动机常常会导致可疑的研究实践（QRP）：不足以构成彻底研究欺诈的不良实践。我们描述了43种可能破坏报告结果的此类做法，并尽可能举例说明。我们的列表强调了在公共基准上对大型语言模型（LLM）的评估。我们还讨论“不可复制的研究实践”，即使其他研究人员难以或不可能复制、发展或审计先前研究的决定。

[NLP-44] A Language Modeling Approach to Diacritic-Free Hebrew TTS
[NLP-44] 无变音希伯来语TTC的语言建模方法

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12206
作者: Amit Roth,Arnon Turetzky,Yossi Adi
关键词: modern Hebrew rarely, modern Hebrew, Hebrew, modern Hebrew results, Hebrew TTS
中文关键词: 现代希伯来语很少，现代希伯来语，希伯来语，现代希伯来语结果，希伯来语https
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: Accepted at Interspeech24

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We tackle the task of text-to-speech (TTS) in Hebrew. Traditional Hebrew contains Diacritics, which dictate the way individuals should pronounce given words, however, modern Hebrew rarely uses them. The lack of diacritics in modern Hebrew results in readers expected to conclude the correct pronunciation and understand which phonemes to use based on the context. This imposes a fundamental challenge on TTS systems to accurately map between text-to-speech. In this work, we propose to adopt a language modeling Diacritics-Free approach, for the task of Hebrew TTS. The model operates on discrete speech representations and is conditioned on a word-piece tokenizer. We optimize the proposed method using in-the-wild weakly supervised data and compare it to several diacritic-based TTS systems. Results suggest the proposed method is superior to the evaluated baselines considering both content preservation and naturalness of the generated speech. Samples can be found under the following link: this http URL
摘要：我们解决了希伯来语的文本到语音(TTS)的任务。传统希伯来语包含变音符号，它规定了个人应该如何发音给定的单词，然而，现代希伯来语很少使用它们。现代希伯来语中没有变音符号，这导致读者希望得出正确的发音，并根据上下文了解应该使用哪些音素。这给TTS系统带来了一个基本的挑战，即在文本到语音之间进行准确的映射。在这项工作中，我们建议采用一种无变音符号的语言建模方法来完成希伯来语TTS的任务。该模型对离散的语音表示进行操作，并以分词标记器为条件。我们使用野外的弱监督数据对所提出的方法进行了优化，并将其与几种基于发音符号的TTS系统进行了比较。结果表明，该方法综合考虑了语音内容保留性和语音的自然性，优于评价基线。可以在以下链接下找到样例：此http URL

[NLP-45] MASIVE: Open-Ended Affective State Identification in English and Spanish
[NLP-45] MASIVE：英语和西班牙语的开放式情感状态识别

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12196
作者: Nicholas Deas,Elsbeth Turcan,Iván Pérez Mejía,Kathleen McKeown
关键词: NLP research focuses, NLP research, discrete emotion categories, research focuses, focuses on identifying
中文关键词: NLP研究重点，NLP研究，离散情感类别，研究重点，重点识别
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In the field of emotion analysis, much NLP research focuses on identifying a limited number of discrete emotion categories, often applied across languages. These basic sets, however, are rarely designed with textual data in mind, and culture, language, and dialect can influence how particular emotions are interpreted. In this work, we broaden our scope to a practically unbounded set of \textitaffective states, which includes any terms that humans use to describe their experiences of feeling. We collect and publish MASIVE, a dataset of Reddit posts in English and Spanish containing over 1,000 unique affective states each. We then define the new problem of \textitaffective state identification for language generation models framed as a masked span prediction task. On this task, we find that smaller finetuned multilingual models outperform much larger LLMs, even on region-specific Spanish affective states. Additionally, we show that pretraining on MASIVE improves model performance on existing emotion benchmarks. Finally, through machine translation experiments, we find that native speaker-written data is vital to good performance on this task.
摘要：在情感分析领域，许多自然语言处理研究集中于识别有限数量的离散情感类别，这些类别通常跨语言应用。然而，这些基本集合很少在设计时考虑到文本数据，并且文化、语言和方言可以影响如何解释特定的情感。在这项工作中，我们将我们的范围扩大到一组几乎无限的文本有效状态，其中包括人类用来描述他们的感觉体验的任何术语。我们收集并发布了MASIVE，这是Reddit英语和西班牙语帖子的数据集，每个帖子包含超过1000种独特的情感状态。然后，我们定义了语言生成模型的文本有效状态识别这一新问题，并将其定义为掩蔽跨度预测任务。在这项任务中，我们发现较小的精调多语言模型的表现优于较大的LLM，即使在特定地区的西班牙语情感状态下也是如此。此外，我们还表明，MASIVE上的预训练提高了模型在现有情绪基准上的性能。最后，通过机器翻译实验，我们发现母语人士书写的数据对于这项任务的良好表现至关重要。

[NLP-46] GPT-4V Cannot Generate Radiology Reports Yet
[NLP-46] GPT-4V尚无法生成放射学报告

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12176
作者: Yuyang Jiang,Chacha Chen,Dang Nguyen,Benjamin M. Mervak,Chenhao Tan
关键词: purported strong multimodal, strong multimodal abilities, multimodal abilities raise, abilities raise interests, purported strong
中文关键词: 号称强大的多模式，强大的多模式能力，多模式能力提高，能力提高兴趣，号称强大
类目: Computers and Society (cs.CY); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 24 pages, 3 figures, code: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:GPT-4V’s purported strong multimodal abilities raise interests in using it to automate radiology report writing, but there lacks thorough evaluations. In this work, we perform a systematic evaluation of GPT-4V in generating radiology reports on two chest X-ray report datasets: MIMIC-CXR and IU X-Ray. We attempt to directly generate reports using GPT-4V through different prompting strategies and find that it fails terribly in both lexical metrics and clinical efficacy metrics. To understand the low performance, we decompose the task into two steps: 1) the medical image reasoning step of predicting medical condition labels from images; and 2) the report synthesis step of generating reports from (groundtruth) conditions. We show that GPT-4V’s performance in image reasoning is consistently low across different prompts. In fact, the distributions of model-predicted labels remain constant regardless of which groundtruth conditions are present on the image, suggesting that the model is not interpreting chest X-rays meaningfully. Even when given groundtruth conditions in report synthesis, its generated reports are less correct and less natural-sounding than a finetuned LLaMA-2. Altogether, our findings cast doubt on the viability of using GPT-4V in a radiology workflow.
摘要：GPT-4V据称具有强大的多模式能力，这引起了人们对使用它自动撰写放射学报告的兴趣，但缺乏全面的评估。在这项工作中，我们在两个胸部X射线报告数据集：MIMIC-CXR和IUX-Ray上对GPT-4V生成放射学报告进行了系统的评估。我们尝试使用GPT-4V通过不同的提示策略直接生成报告，结果发现它在词汇指标和临床疗效指标上都失败了。为了理解性能低下的原因，我们将任务分解为两个步骤：1)从图像预测医疗状况标签的医学图像推理步骤；2)从(基本事实)状况生成报告的报告合成步骤。我们发现，在不同的提示下，GPT-4V在图像推理中的表现一直较低。事实上，无论图像上存在哪些基本真实条件，模型预测的标签的分布都保持不变，这表明该模型没有对胸部X光进行有意义的解释。即使在报告合成中给定了基本事实条件，其生成的报告也不如精调的骆驼-2那么准确，听起来也不那么自然。总之，我们的发现对在放射学工作流程中使用GPT-4V的可行性提出了质疑。

[NLP-47] Predicting Emotion Intensity in Polish Political Texts: Comparing Supervised Models and Large Language Models in a Resource-Poor Language
[NLP-47] 预测波兰政治文本中的情感强度：比较资源匮乏语言中的监督模型和大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12141
作者: Hubert Plisiecki,Piotr Koc,Maria Flakus,Artur Pokropek
关键词: Polish political texts, resource-poor language context, Polish political, predict emotion intensity, large language models
中文关键词: 波兰政治文本，资源匮乏的语言背景，波兰政治，预测情感强度，大型语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: The Appendix is located at the very bottom of the manuscript

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study explores the use of large language models (LLMs) to predict emotion intensity in Polish political texts, a resource-poor language context. The research compares the performance of several LLMs against a supervised model trained on an annotated corpus of 10,000 social media texts, evaluated for the intensity of emotions by expert judges. The findings indicate that while the supervised model generally outperforms LLMs, offering higher accuracy and lower variance, LLMs present a viable alternative, especially given the high costs associated with data annotation. The study highlights the potential of LLMs in low-resource language settings and underscores the need for further research on emotion intensity prediction and its application across different languages and continuous features. The implications suggest a nuanced decision-making process to choose the right approach to emotion prediction for researchers and practitioners based on resource availability and the specific requirements of their tasks.
摘要：本研究探讨了使用大语言模型(LLM)来预测波兰政治语篇中的情感强度。这项研究将几个LLMS与一个监督模型的性能进行了比较，该模型在10000个带有注释的社交媒体文本语料库上进行了训练，专家评委对其进行了情感强度评估。研究结果表明，虽然监督模型总体上优于最小二乘法，提供了更高的精度和更低的方差，但最小二乘法提供了一个可行的替代方案，特别是考虑到与数据标注相关的高成本。这项研究强调了LLMS在低资源语言环境中的潜力，并强调了进一步研究情绪强度预测的必要性，以及它在不同语言和连续特征中的应用。这些暗示暗示了一个细微差别的决策过程，以根据资源的可用性和他们任务的具体要求为研究人员和从业者选择正确的情绪预测方法。

[NLP-48] LLMs-in-the-loop Part-1: Expert Small AI Models for Bio-Medical Text Translation
[NLP-48] LLM在环第1部分：生物医学文本翻译的专家小型人工智能模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12126
作者: Bunyamin Keles,Murat Gunay,Serdar I.Caglar
关键词: indispensable in healthcare, healthcare for enabling, enabling the global, global dissemination, Machine translation
中文关键词: 医疗保健中不可或缺，医疗保健用于实现，实现全球、全球传播，机器翻译
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 14 pages, 2 figures, 9 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Machine translation is indispensable in healthcare for enabling the global dissemination of medical knowledge across languages. However, complex medical terminology poses unique challenges to achieving adequate translation quality and accuracy. This study introduces a novel “LLMs-in-the-loop” approach to develop supervised neural machine translation models optimized specifically for medical texts. While large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated powerful capabilities, this research shows that small, specialized models trained on high-quality in-domain (mostly synthetic) data can outperform even vastly larger LLMs. Custom parallel corpora in six languages were compiled from scientific articles, synthetically generated clinical documents, and medical texts. Our LLMs-in-the-loop methodology employs synthetic data generation, rigorous evaluation, and agent orchestration to enhance performance. We developed small medical translation models using the MarianMT base model. We introduce a new medical translation test dataset to standardize evaluation in this domain. Assessed using BLEU, METEOR, ROUGE, and BERT scores on this test set, our MarianMT-based models outperform Google Translate, DeepL, and GPT-4-Turbo. Results demonstrate that our LLMs-in-the-loop approach, combined with fine-tuning high-quality, domain-specific data, enables specialized models to outperform general-purpose and some larger systems. This research, part of a broader series on expert small models, paves the way for future healthcare-related AI developments, including deidentification and bio-medical entity extraction models. Our study underscores the potential of tailored neural translation models and the LLMs-in-the-loop methodology to advance the field through improved data generation, evaluation, agent, and modeling techniques. Comments: 14 pages, 2 figures, 9 tables Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI) MSC classes: 68T35 Cite as: arXiv:2407.12126 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2407.12126v1 [cs.CL] for this version)
摘要：机器翻译在医疗保健领域是不可或缺的，因为它能够在全球范围内跨语言传播医学知识。然而，复杂的医学术语对实现足够的翻译质量和准确性提出了独特的挑战。本研究介绍了一种新的“LLMS-in-the-loop”方法来开发专门针对医学文本优化的有监督神经机器翻译模型。虽然大型语言模型(LLM)已经显示出强大的能力，但这项研究表明，基于高质量领域内(主要是合成的)数据训练的小型专业模型可以胜过更大的LLM。6种语言的定制平行语料库是从科学文章、人工生成的临床文献和医学文本中汇编而成的。我们的LLMS-in-the-loop方法采用合成数据生成、严格评估和代理协调来提高性能。我们使用MarianMT基础模型开发了小型医学翻译模型。我们引入了一个新的医学翻译测试数据集来规范这一领域的评估。使用BLEU、流星、胭脂和伯特在这一测试集上的分数进行评估，我们基于MarianMT的模型的表现优于谷歌翻译、DeepL和GPT-4-Turbo。结果表明，我们的LLMS-in-Loop方法，结合微调的高质量、特定于领域的数据，使专业模型的表现优于通用系统和一些较大的系统。这项研究是关于专家小模型的更广泛系列的一部分，为未来与医疗保健相关的人工智能发展铺平了道路，包括身份识别和生物医疗实体提取模型。我们的研究强调了定制神经翻译模型和LLMS-in-the-Loop方法通过改进数据生成、评估、代理和建模技术来推动该领域发展的潜力。评论：14页，2图，9表主题：计算与语言(cs.CL)；人工智能(cs.AI)理科课程：68T35引用AS：arxiv：2407.12126cs.CL

[NLP-49] Private prediction for large-scale synthetic text generation
[NLP-49] 用于大规模合成文本生成的私人预测

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12108
作者: Kareem Amin,Alex Bie,Weiwei Kong,Alexey Kurakin,Natalia Ponomareva,Umar Syed,Andreas Terzis,Sergei Vassilvitskii
关键词: differentially private synthetic, present an approach, large language models, generating differentially private, differential privacy guarantees
中文关键词: 差异隐私合成，提出一种方法，大型语言模型，生成差异隐私，差异隐私保证
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Cryptography and Security (cs.CR)
备注: 12 pages main text + 15 pages appendix

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present an approach for generating differentially private synthetic text using large language models (LLMs), via private prediction. In the private prediction framework, we only require the output synthetic data to satisfy differential privacy guarantees. This is in contrast to approaches that train a generative model on potentially sensitive user-supplied source data and seek to ensure the model itself is safe to release. We prompt a pretrained LLM with source data, but ensure that next-token predictions are made with differential privacy guarantees. Previous work in this paradigm reported generating a small number of examples (10) at reasonable privacy levels, an amount of data that is useful only for downstream in-context learning or prompting. In contrast, we make changes that allow us to generate thousands of high-quality synthetic data points, greatly expanding the set of potential applications. Our improvements come from an improved privacy analysis and a better private selection mechanism, which makes use of the equivalence between the softmax layer for sampling tokens in LLMs and the exponential mechanism. Furthermore, we introduce a novel use of public predictions via the sparse vector technique, in which we do not pay privacy costs for tokens that are predictable without sensitive data; we find this to be particularly effective for structured data. Comments: 12 pages main text + 15 pages appendix Subjects: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Cryptography and Security (cs.CR) Cite as: arXiv:2407.12108 [cs.LG] (or arXiv:2407.12108v1 [cs.LG] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2407.12108 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite
摘要：我们提出了一种利用大型语言模型(LLMS)，通过私密预测来生成差异私密合成文本的方法。在私有预测框架中，我们只要求输出的合成数据满足不同的隐私保证。这与在潜在敏感的用户提供的源数据上训练生成性模型并寻求确保模型本身可以安全发布的方法形成了鲜明对比。我们使用源数据提示预先训练的LLM，但确保使用不同的隐私保证进行下一个令牌预测。这一范例以前的工作报告说，在合理的隐私水平下生成少量的示例(10)，这些数据量仅对下游的情景中学习或提示有用。相比之下，我们进行的更改允许我们生成数千个高质量的合成数据点，从而极大地扩展了潜在应用程序的集合。我们的改进来自于改进的隐私分析和更好的私有选择机制，该机制利用了LLMS中用于采样令牌的Softmax层与指数机制之间的等价性。此外，我们通过稀疏向量技术引入了公共预测的新用法，其中我们不为在没有敏感数据的情况下可预测的令牌支付隐私成本；我们发现这对结构化数据特别有效。评论：12页正文+15页附录主题：机器学习(cs.LG)；计算和语言(cs.CL)；密码学和安全(cs.CR)引用为：arxiv：2407.12108cs.lghttps://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2407.12108 Focus通过DataCite了解更多arxiv发布的文档

[NLP-50] Better RAG using Relevant Information Gain
[NLP-50] 使用相关信息收益更好的RAG

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12101
作者: Marc Pickett,Jeremy Hartman,Ayan Kumar Bhowmick,Raquib-ul Alam,Aditya Vempaty
关键词: LLM context window, large language models, inserts text retrieved, LLM context, context window
中文关键词: LLM上下文窗口、大型语言模型、插入检索的文本、LLM上下文、上下文窗口
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 4 page paper submitted to EMNLP

点击查看摘要

Abstract:A common way to extend the memory of large language models (LLMs) is by retrieval augmented generation (RAG), which inserts text retrieved from a larger memory into an LLM’s context window. However, the context window is typically limited to several thousand tokens, which limits the number of retrieved passages that can inform a model’s response. For this reason, it’s important to avoid occupying context window space with redundant information by ensuring a degree of diversity among retrieved passages. At the same time, the information should also be relevant to the current task. Most prior methods that encourage diversity among retrieved results, such as Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR), do so by incorporating an objective that explicitly trades off diversity and relevance. We propose a novel simple optimization metric based on relevant information gain, a probabilistic measure of the total information relevant to a query for a set of retrieved results. By optimizing this metric, diversity organically emerges from our system. When used as a drop-in replacement for the retrieval component of a RAG system, this method yields state-of-the-art performance on question answering tasks from the Retrieval Augmented Generation Benchmark (RGB), outperforming existing metrics that directly optimize for relevance and diversity.
摘要：扩展大型语言模型(LLMS)存储空间的一种常见方法是检索增强生成(RAG)，它将从较大存储空间中检索到的文本插入到LLM的上下文窗口中。然而，上下文窗口通常被限制为数千个令牌，这限制了可以通知模型响应的检索到的段落的数量。因此，通过确保检索到的段落之间的一定程度的多样性，避免使用冗余信息来占用上下文窗口空间是很重要的。同时，这些信息也应该与当前任务相关。大多数以前鼓励检索结果多样性的方法，如最大边际相关性(MMR)，都是通过加入明确权衡多样性和相关性的目标来实现的。我们提出了一种新的基于相关信息增益的简单优化度量，该度量是对与一组检索结果的查询相关的全部信息的概率度量。通过优化这一指标，多样性从我们的系统中有机地出现。当用作RAG系统的检索组件的替代组件时，该方法在检索增强生成基准(RGB)的问答任务上产生最先进的性能，表现优于直接优化相关性和多样性的现有指标。

[NLP-51] Identifying Speakers in Dialogue Transcripts: A Text-based Approach Using Pretrained Language Models
[NLP-51] 识别对话笔录中的发言者：使用预训练语言模型的基于文本的方法

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12094
作者: Minh Nguyen,Franck Dernoncourt,Seunghyun Yoon,Hanieh Deilamsalehy,Hao Tan,Ryan Rossi,Quan Hung Tran,Trung Bui,Thien Huu Nguyen
关键词: enhancing content accessibility, digital media archives, introduce an approach, approach to identifying, enhancing content
中文关键词: 增强内容可访问性、数字媒体档案、引入一种方法、识别、增强内容的方法
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: accepted to INTERSPEECH 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We introduce an approach to identifying speaker names in dialogue transcripts, a crucial task for enhancing content accessibility and searchability in digital media archives. Despite the advancements in speech recognition, the task of text-based speaker identification (SpeakerID) has received limited attention, lacking large-scale, diverse datasets for effective model training. Addressing these gaps, we present a novel, large-scale dataset derived from the MediaSum corpus, encompassing transcripts from a wide range of media sources. We propose novel transformer-based models tailored for SpeakerID, leveraging contextual cues within dialogues to accurately attribute speaker names. Through extensive experiments, our best model achieves a great precision of 80.3%, setting a new benchmark for SpeakerID. The data and code are publicly available here: \urlthis https URL
摘要：我们引入了一种识别对话笔录中发言人姓名的方法，这是增强数字媒体档案中内容可访问性和可搜索性的关键任务。尽管语音识别取得了进步，但基于文本的说话人识别（SpeakerID）任务受到的关注有限，缺乏大规模、多样化的数据集来进行有效的模型训练。为了解决这些差距，我们提出了一个源自MediaSum文集的新颖、大规模数据集，其中涵盖了来自广泛媒体来源的文字记录。我们提出了专为SpeakerID量身定制的新型基于转换器的模型，利用对话中的上下文线索来准确地归属说话者姓名。通过大量实验，我们的最佳模型实现了80.3%的高精度，为SpeakerID树立了新的基准。数据和代码可在此处公开：\urlThis https URL

[NLP-52] GoldFinch: High Performance RWKV/Transformer Hybrid with Linear Pre-Fill and Extreme KV-Cache Compression
[NLP-52] GoldFinch：具有线性预填充和极端KV-缓存压缩的高性能RWKV/Transformer混合体

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12077
作者: Daniel Goldstein,Fares Obeid,Eric Alcaide,Guangyu Song,Eugene Cheah
关键词: hybrid Linear Attention, hybrid Linear, Transformer sequence model, Linear Attention, technique to efficiently
中文关键词: 混合线性注意力、混合线性、Transformer序列模型、线性注意力、高效技术
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We introduce GoldFinch, a hybrid Linear Attention/Transformer sequence model that uses a new technique to efficiently generate a highly compressed and reusable KV-Cache in linear time and space with respect to sequence length. GoldFinch stacks our new GOLD transformer on top of an enhanced version of the Finch (RWKV-6) architecture. We train up to 1.5B parameter class models of the Finch, Llama, and GoldFinch architectures, and find dramatically improved modeling performance relative to both Finch and Llama. Our cache size savings increase linearly with model layer count, ranging from 756-2550 times smaller than the traditional transformer cache for common sizes, enabling inference of extremely large context lengths even on limited hardware. Although autoregressive generation has O(n) time complexity per token because of attention, pre-fill computation of the entire initial cache state for a submitted context costs only O(1) time per token due to the use of a recurrent neural network (RNN) to generate this cache. We release our trained weights and training code under the Apache 2.0 license for community use.
摘要：我们介绍了Goldfinch，一种混合的线性注意/变形者序列模型，它使用一种新的技术来高效地在线性时间和空间中生成关于序列长度的高度压缩和可复用的KV-Cache。Goldfinch将我们新的黄金变压器堆叠在Finch(RWKV-6)架构的增强版本上。我们训练了多达15B的Finch、Llama和Goldfinch体系结构的参数类模型，并发现相对于Finch和Llama体系结构的建模性能有了显著提高。我们节省的高速缓存大小随着模型层数的增加而线性增加，一般大小的高速缓存比传统变压器高速缓存小756-2550倍，即使在有限的硬件上也能推断出极大的上下文长度。尽管由于注意力的原因，自回归生成对于每个令牌具有O(N)时间复杂度，但是由于使用递归神经网络(RNN)来生成该高速缓存，针对所提交的上下文的整个初始高速缓存状态的预填充计算仅花费每个令牌O(1)时间。我们在Apache2.0许可下发布我们训练的重量和训练代码，供社区使用。

[NLP-53] NinjaLLM: Fast Scalable and Cost-effective RAG using Amazon SageMaker and AWS Trainium and Inferentia2
[NLP-53] NinjaLLM：使用Amazon SageMaker和AWS Trainium和Infertia 2快速扩展且经济实惠的RAG

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12057
作者: Tengfei Xue,Xuefeng Li,Roman Smirnov,Tahir Azim,Arash Sadrieh,Babak Pahlavan
关键词: Retrieval-augmented generation, traditional RAG techniques, conversational format, widely used today, today to retrieve
中文关键词: 检索增强生成、传统RAG技术、对话格式，今天广泛使用，今天用于检索
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) techniques are widely used today to retrieve and present information in a conversational format. This paper presents a set of enhancements to traditional RAG techniques, focusing on large language models (LLMs) fine-tuned and hosted on AWS Trainium and Inferentia2 AI chips via SageMaker. These chips are characterized by their elasticity, affordability, and efficient performance for AI compute tasks. Besides enabling deployment on these chips, this work aims to improve tool usage, add citation capabilities, and mitigate the risks of hallucinations and unsafe responses due to context bias. We benchmark our RAG system’s performance on the Natural Questions and HotPotQA datasets, achieving an accuracy of 62% and 59% respectively, exceeding other models such as DBRX and Mixtral Instruct.
摘要：检索增强生成（RAG）技术如今被广泛用于以对话格式检索和呈现信息。本文介绍了对传统RAG技术的一系列增强功能，重点关注通过SageMaker在AWS Trainium和Infertia 2 AI芯片上进行微调和托管的大型语言模型（LLM）。这些芯片的特点是灵活性、经济实惠性和可执行人工智能计算任务的高效性能。除了能够在这些芯片上部署之外，这项工作还旨在提高工具的使用率、添加引用功能，并减轻由于上下文偏见而导致的幻觉和不安全反应的风险。我们在Natural Questions和HotPotQA数据集上对RAG系统的性能进行了基准测试，分别实现了62%和59%的准确性，超过了DBRx和Mixtral Direct等其他模型。

[NLP-54] he Art of Saying No: Contextual Noncompliance in Language Models
[NLP-54] 说不的艺术：语言模型中的上下文不合规

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12043
作者: Faeze Brahman,Sachin Kumar,Vidhisha Balachandran,Pradeep Dasigi,Valentina Pyatkin,Abhilasha Ravichander,Sarah Wiegreffe,Nouha Dziri,Khyathi Chandu,Jack Hessel,Yulia Tsvetkov,Noah A. Smith,Yejin Choi,Hannaneh Hajishirzi
关键词: Chat-based language models, Chat-based language, Chat-based, models, requests
中文关键词: 基于聊天的语言模型，基于聊天的语言，基于聊天的，模型，请求
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Chat-based language models are designed to be helpful, yet they should not comply with every user request. While most existing work primarily focuses on refusal of “unsafe” queries, we posit that the scope of noncompliance should be broadened. We introduce a comprehensive taxonomy of contextual noncompliance describing when and how models should not comply with user requests. Our taxonomy spans a wide range of categories including incomplete, unsupported, indeterminate, and humanizing requests (in addition to unsafe requests). To test noncompliance capabilities of language models, we use this taxonomy to develop a new evaluation suite of 1000 noncompliance prompts. We find that most existing models show significantly high compliance rates in certain previously understudied categories with models like GPT-4 incorrectly complying with as many as 30% of requests. To address these gaps, we explore different training strategies using a synthetically-generated training set of requests and expected noncompliant responses. Our experiments demonstrate that while direct finetuning of instruction-tuned models can lead to both over-refusal and a decline in general capabilities, using parameter efficient methods like low rank adapters helps to strike a good balance between appropriate noncompliance and other capabilities.
摘要：基于聊天的语言模型被设计为有用的，但它们不应该遵从每个用户的请求。虽然大多数现有的工作主要集中在拒绝“不安全”的询问上，但我们假设不遵守的范围应该扩大。我们引入了一个全面的上下文不遵从分类，描述了模型何时以及如何不符合用户请求。我们的分类涵盖了广泛的类别，包括不完整、不支持、不确定和人性化的请求(除了不安全的请求之外)。为了测试语言模型的不合规能力，我们使用这个分类来开发一个包含1000个不合规提示的新评估套件。我们发现，大多数现有的模型在某些以前研究不足的类别中显示出相当高的遵从率，像GPT-4这样的模型错误地遵守了高达30%的请求。为了弥补这些差距，我们使用综合生成的请求和预期不合规响应的训练集来探索不同的培训策略。我们的实验表明，虽然直接微调指令调优模型会导致过度拒绝和一般能力的下降，但使用参数高效的方法，如低阶适配器，有助于在适当的非遵从性和其他能力之间取得良好的平衡。

[NLP-55] Exploring Advanced Large Language Models with LLMsuite
[NLP-55] 使用LLMsuite探索高级大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12036
作者: Giorgio Roffo
关键词: Large Language Models, Program-Aided Language Models, Language Models, Large Language, ChatGPT and Gemini
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、程序辅助语言模型、语言模型、大型语言、ChatGPT和Gemini
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: Keywords: Language Model Benchmarking, Pre-Trained LLM Comparison, LLM Performance Analysis, NLP Model Evaluation Tools, Public Dataset Inference for LLMs, BLEU and ROUGE Metrics for LLM, Open Source LLM Testing Tools, Large Language Model Evaluation Software, NLP Benchmarking Suite, Comprehensive LLM Evaluation Toolkit

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This tutorial explores the advancements and challenges in the development of Large Language Models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT and Gemini. It addresses inherent limitations like temporal knowledge cutoffs, mathematical inaccuracies, and the generation of incorrect information, proposing solutions like Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Program-Aided Language Models (PAL), and frameworks such as ReAct and LangChain. The integration of these techniques enhances LLM performance and reliability, especially in multi-step reasoning and complex task execution. The paper also covers fine-tuning strategies, including instruction fine-tuning, parameter-efficient methods like LoRA, and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) as well as Reinforced Self-Training (ReST). Additionally, it provides a comprehensive survey of transformer architectures and training techniques for LLMs. The toolbox for implementing these techniques is publicly available at this https URL
摘要：本教程探讨了ChatGPT和Gemini等大型语言模型（LLM）开发中的进步和挑战。它解决了时态知识截止、数学不准确和不正确信息的生成等固有限制，提出了检索增强生成（RAG）、程序辅助语言模型（PAL）以及ReAct和LangChain等框架等解决方案。这些技术的集成增强了LLM的性能和可靠性，特别是在多步骤推理和复杂任务执行方面。该论文还涵盖了微调策略，包括指令微调、LoRA等参数高效方法、人类反馈强化学习（RL HF）以及强化自我训练（ReST）。此外，它还提供了对LLM的Transformer架构和培训技术的全面调查。用于实现这些技术的工具箱可在此https URL中公开获取

[NLP-56] Understanding Transformers via N-gram Statistics
[NLP-56] 通过N-gram统计了解变形金刚

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12034
作者: Timothy Nguyen
关键词: display extreme proficiency, work remains elusive, based large-language models, Transformer based large-language, large-language models
中文关键词: 显示极其熟练，工作仍然难以捉摸，基于大语言模型，基于Transformer的大语言，大语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Transformer based large-language models (LLMs) display extreme proficiency with language yet a precise understanding of how they work remains elusive. One way of demystifying transformer predictions would be to describe how they depend on their context in terms of simple template functions. This paper takes a first step in this direction by considering families of functions (i.e. rules) formed out of simple N-gram based statistics of the training data. By studying how well these rulesets approximate transformer predictions, we obtain a variety of novel discoveries: a simple method to detect overfitting during training without using a holdout set, a quantitative measure of how transformers progress from learning simple to more complex statistical rules over the course of training, a model-variance criterion governing when transformer predictions tend to be described by N-gram rules, and insights into how well transformers can be approximated by N-gram rulesets in the limit where these rulesets become increasingly complex. In this latter direction, we find that for 78% of LLM next-token distributions on TinyStories, their top-1 predictions agree with those provided by our N-gram rulesets.
摘要：基于转换器的大语言模型(LLM)显示出对语言的极度熟练，但对其工作原理的准确理解仍然难以捉摸。揭开变压器预测的神秘面纱的一种方法是用简单的模板函数描述它们如何依赖于它们的上下文。本文在这个方向上迈出了第一步，通过考虑由简单的基于N元语法的训练数据统计形成的函数族(即规则)。通过研究这些规则集对变压器预测的逼近程度，我们获得了各种新的发现：一种简单的方法来检测训练过程中的过度匹配，而不使用抗拒集，对变压器在训练过程中如何从学习简单的统计规则到更复杂的统计规则的量化度量，管理何时用N元语法规则描述变压器预测的模型方差标准，以及在这些规则集变得越来越复杂的极限情况下用N元语法规则集逼近变压器的能力。在后一个方向上，我们发现对于TinyStories上78%的LLM Next-Token分发，他们的TOP-1预测与我们的N-gram规则集提供的预测一致。

[NLP-57] reeSeg: Hierarchical Topic Segmentation of Large Transcripts
[NLP-57] reeSeg：大型成绩单的分层主题分割

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12028
作者: Dimitrios C. Gklezakos,Timothy Misiak,Diamond Bishop
关键词: commoditized Large Language, Large Language Models, Automatic Speech Recognition, organizing recorded videos, Language Models
中文关键词: 商品化大语言、大语言模型、自动语音识别、组织录制的视频、语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:From organizing recorded videos and meetings into chapters, to breaking down large inputs in order to fit them into the context window of commoditized Large Language Models (LLMs), topic segmentation of large transcripts emerges as a task of increasing significance. Still, accurate segmentation presents many challenges, including (a) the noisy nature of the Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) software typically used to obtain the transcripts, (b) the lack of diverse labeled data and © the difficulty in pin-pointing the ground-truth number of segments. In this work we present TreeSeg, an approach that combines off-the-shelf embedding models with divisive clustering, to generate hierarchical, structured segmentations of transcripts in the form of binary trees. Our approach is robust to noise and can handle large transcripts efficiently. We evaluate TreeSeg on the ICSI and AMI corpora, demonstrating that it outperforms all baselines. Finally, we introduce TinyRec, a small-scale corpus of manually annotated transcripts, obtained from self-recorded video sessions.
摘要：从将录制的视频和会议组织成章节，到将大量输入分解为适合商品化的大型语言模型(LLMS)的上下文窗口，大型文本的主题分割成为一项日益重要的任务。尽管如此，准确的分割仍然面临许多挑战，包括(A)通常用于获得成绩单的自动语音识别(ASR)软件的噪声性质，(B)缺乏多样化的标记数据，以及©难以准确地确定片段的真实数量。在这项工作中，我们提出了TreeSeg，一种将现成的嵌入模型与分裂聚类相结合的方法，以二叉树的形式生成分层的、结构化的转录片段。我们的方法对噪声是稳健的，并且可以有效地处理大的转录。我们在ICSI和AMI语料库上对TreeSeg进行了评估，表明它的表现优于所有基线。最后，我们介绍了TinyRec，一个小规模的人工注释转录的语料库，从自我录制的视频会话中获得。

[NLP-58] he Pitfalls of Publishing in the Age of LLMs: Strange and Surprising Adventures with a High-Impact NLP Journal
[NLP-58] LLM时代的出版陷阱：高影响力NLP期刊的奇怪而令人惊讶的冒险

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12026
作者: Rakesh M. Verma,Nachum Dershowitz
关键词: academic publishing realm, recent case study, NLP journal, show the fraught, fraught side
中文关键词: 学术出版领域、最近的案例研究、NLP期刊展示了令人担忧的一面
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We show the fraught side of the academic publishing realm and illustrate it through a recent case study with an NLP journal.
摘要：我们展示了学术出版领域令人担忧的一面，并通过最近NLP期刊的案例研究来说明这一点。

[NLP-59] CMMaTH: A Chinese Multi-modal Math Skill Evaluation Benchmark for Foundation Models
[NLP-59] CMMaTH：中国基础模型多模式数学技能评估基准

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12023
作者: Zhong-Zhi Li,Ming-Liang Zhang,Fei Yin,Zhi-Long Ji,Jin-Feng Bai,Zhen-Ru Pan,Fan-Hu Zeng,Jian Xu,Jia-Xin Zhang,Cheng-Lin Liu
关键词: receive wide attention, Chinese multimodal mathematical, mathematical capabilities continues, multimodal mathematical capabilities, multimodal mathematical
中文关键词: 受到广泛关注，中国多模式数学，数学能力继续，多模式数学能力，多模式数学
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Due to the rapid advancements in multimodal large language models, evaluating their multimodal mathematical capabilities continues to receive wide attention. Despite the datasets like MathVista proposed benchmarks for assessing mathematical capabilities in multimodal scenarios, there is still a lack of corresponding evaluation tools and datasets for fine-grained assessment in the context of K12 education in Chinese language. To systematically evaluate the capability of multimodal large models in solving Chinese multimodal mathematical problems, we propose a Chinese Multi-modal Math Skill Evaluation Benchmark, named CMMaTH, contraining 23k multimodal K12 math related questions, forming the largest Chinese multimodal mathematical problem benchmark to date. CMMaTH questions from elementary to high school levels, provide increased diversity in problem types, solution objectives, visual elements, detailed knowledge points, and standard solution annotations. We have constructed an open-source tool GradeGPT integrated with the CMMaTH dataset, facilitating stable, rapid, and cost-free model evaluation. Our data and code are available.
摘要：随着多通道大语言模型的快速发展，评价其多通道数学能力继续受到广泛关注。尽管MathVista等数据集提出了多通道情景下数学能力评估的基准，但在中文K12教育背景下，仍缺乏相应的评估工具和数据集用于细粒度评估。为了系统地评价多通道大型模型解决中文多通道数学问题的能力，我们针对23K个多通道K12数学问题，提出了一个中文多通道数学技能评估基准CMMaTH，形成了迄今为止最大的中文多通道数学问题基准。从小学到高中的CMMaTH问题，在问题类型、解决方案目标、视觉元素、详细的知识点和标准解决方案注释方面提供了更多的多样性。我们构建了一个与CMMaTH数据集集成的开源工具GradeGPT，便于稳定、快速和免费的模型评估。我们的数据和代码都是可用的。

[NLP-60] ITERTL: An Iterative Framework for Fine-tuning LLMs for RTL Code Generation
[NLP-60] ITERTL：用于微调RTL代码生成的LLM的迭代框架

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12022
作者: Peiyang Wu,Nan Guo,Xiao Xiao,Wenming Li,Xiaochun Ye,Dongrui Fan
关键词: demonstrated excellent performance, understanding human instructions, generating RTL code, RTL code, RTL codes typically
中文关键词: 表现出出色的性能，理解人类指令，生成RTL代码，RTL代码，RTL代码通常
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recently, large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated excellent performance in understanding human instructions and generating code, which has inspired researchers to explore the feasibility of generating RTL code with LLMs. However, the existing approaches to fine-tune LLMs on RTL codes typically are conducted on fixed datasets, which do not fully stimulate the capability of LLMs and require large amounts of reference data. To mitigate these issues , we introduce a simple yet effective iterative training paradigm named ITERTL. During each iteration, samples are drawn from the model trained in the previous cycle. Then these new samples are employed for training in this loop. Through this iterative approach, the distribution mismatch between the model and the training samples is reduced. Additionally, the model is thus enabled to explore a broader generative space and receive more comprehensive feedback. Theoretical analyses are conducted to investigate the mechanism of the effectiveness. Experimental results show the model trained through our proposed approach can compete with and even outperform the state-of-the-art (SOTA) open-source model with nearly 37% reference samples, achieving remarkable 42.9% and 62.2% pass@1 rate on two VerilogEval evaluation datasets respectively. While using the same amount of reference samples, our method can achieved a relative improvement of 16.9% and 12.5% in pass@1 compared to the non-iterative method. This study facilitates the application of LLMs for generating RTL code in practical scenarios with limited data.
摘要：近年来，大型语言模型在理解人类指令和生成代码方面表现出了优异的性能，这促使研究人员探索用大型语言模型生成RTL代码的可行性。然而，现有的在RTL代码上微调LLM的方法通常是在固定的数据集上进行的，这不能充分激发LLM的能力，并且需要大量的参考数据。为了缓解这些问题，我们引入了一种简单但有效的迭代训练范例，名为ITERTL。在每次迭代期间，从前一个周期中训练的模型中提取样本。然后使用这些新样本在该环路中进行训练。通过这种迭代方法，减少了模型和训练样本之间的分布失配。此外，该模型因此能够探索更广阔的生成空间，并获得更全面的反馈。并进行了理论分析，探讨了其有效性的机理。实验结果表明，在两个VerilogEval评测数据集上，使用该方法训练的模型可以与拥有近37个参考样本的最新开源模型竞争甚至超越，分别获得了42.9%和62.2%的通过率。在使用相同数量的参考样本的情况下，与非迭代方法相比，我们的方法可以获得16.9%和12.5%的PASS@1的相对改进。这项研究有助于在数据有限的实际场景中使用LLMS生成RTL代码。

[NLP-61] Adaptive Draft-Verification for Efficient Large Language Model Decoding
[NLP-61] 高效大语言模型解码的自适应草稿验证

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12021
作者: Xukun Liu,Bowen Lei,Ruqi Zhang,Dongkuan Xu
关键词: Large language model, Large language, model learned probabilities, generating a sequence, decoding involves generating
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，大型语言，模型学习概率，生成序列，解码涉及生成
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: Under review of Neurips 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language model (LLM) decoding involves generating a sequence of tokens based on a given context, where each token is predicted one at a time using the model’s learned probabilities. The typical autoregressive decoding method requires a separate forward pass through the model for each token generated, which is computationally inefficient and poses challenges for deploying LLMs in latency-sensitive scenarios. The main limitations of current decoding methods stem from their inefficiencies and resource demands. Existing approaches either necessitate fine-tuning smaller models, which is resource-intensive, or rely on fixed retrieval schemes to construct drafts for the next tokens, which lack adaptability and fail to generalize across different models and contexts. To address these issues, we introduce a novel methodology called ADED, which accelerates LLM decoding without requiring fine-tuning. Our approach involves an adaptive draft-verification process that evolves over time to improve efficiency. We utilize a tri-gram matrix-based LLM representation to dynamically approximate the output distribution of the LLM, allowing the model to adjust to changing token probabilities during the decoding process. Additionally, we implement a draft construction mechanism that effectively balances exploration and exploitation, ensuring that the drafts generated are both diverse and close to the true output distribution of the LLM. The importance of this design lies in its ability to optimize the draft distribution adaptively, leading to faster and more accurate decoding. Through extensive experiments on various benchmark datasets and LLM architectures, we demonstrate that ADED significantly accelerates the decoding process while maintaining high accuracy, making it suitable for deployment in a wide range of practical applications.
摘要：大型语言模型(LLM)解码涉及基于给定上下文生成一系列令牌，其中每个令牌使用模型的学习概率一次预测一个。典型的自回归解码方法需要对每个生成的令牌进行单独的前向遍历，这在计算上效率很低，并且给在延迟敏感场景中部署LLMS带来了挑战。当前解码方法的主要局限性源于它们的低效率和资源需求。现有的方法要么需要微调较小的模型，这是资源密集型的，要么依赖固定的检索方案来构建下一个令牌的草稿，这缺乏适应性，无法在不同的模型和上下文中推广。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了一种称为ED的新方法，该方法在不需要微调的情况下加速了LLM解码。我们的办法涉及一个随时间发展以提高效率的适应性草案核查过程。我们利用基于三元组矩阵的LLM表示法来动态逼近LLM的输出分布，允许模型在解码过程中根据令牌概率的变化进行调整。此外，我们实施了一种有效平衡勘探和开采的草案构建机制，确保产生的草案既多样化，又接近LLM的真实产出分布。这种设计的重要性在于它能够自适应地优化草稿分配，从而导致更快和更准确的解码。通过在不同的基准数据集和LLM架构上的大量实验，我们证明了该算法在保持较高精度的同时显著加快了译码过程，使其适合在广泛的实际应用中部署。

[NLP-62] SignSpeak: Open-Source Time Series Classification for ASL Translation
[NLP-62] SignSpeak：用于ADL翻译的开源时间序列分类

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12020
作者: Aditya Makkar,Divya Makkar,Aarav Patel,Liam Hebert
关键词: sign language remains, speech-impaired communities, sign language, lack of fluency, remains a barrier
中文关键词: 手语仍然存在，言语障碍社区，手语，缺乏流利性，仍然是一个障碍
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 6 pages, 2 figures, NeurIPS

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The lack of fluency in sign language remains a barrier to seamless communication for hearing and speech-impaired communities. In this work, we propose a low-cost, real-time ASL-to-speech translation glove and an exhaustive training dataset of sign language patterns. We then benchmarked this dataset with supervised learning models, such as LSTMs, GRUs and Transformers, where our best model achieved 92% accuracy. The SignSpeak dataset has 7200 samples encompassing 36 classes (A-Z, 1-10) and aims to capture realistic signing patterns by using five low-cost flex sensors to measure finger positions at each time step at 36 Hz. Our open-source dataset, models and glove designs, provide an accurate and efficient ASL translator while maintaining cost-effectiveness, establishing a framework for future work to build on.
摘要：手语缺乏流利仍然是听力和言语障碍社区无缝沟通的障碍。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种低成本、实时的手语转语音翻译手套和一个详尽的手语模式训练数据集。然后，我们使用监督学习模型（例如LSTM、GROUP和Transformers）对该数据集进行了基准测试，其中我们的最佳模型达到了92%的准确率。SignSpeak数据集有7200个样本，涵盖36个类别（A-Z，1-10），旨在通过使用五个低成本柔性传感器在36 Hz下测量每个时间步的手指位置来捕捉真实的签名模式。我们的开源数据集、模型和手套设计提供了准确有效的ATL翻译器，同时保持成本效益，为未来的工作建立了一个框架。

[NLP-63] DIM: Dynamic Integration of Multimodal Entity Linking with Large Language Model
[NLP-63] DIM：多模式实体链接与大型语言模型的动态集成

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12019
作者: Shezheng Song,Shasha Li,Jie Yu,Shan Zhao,Xiaopeng Li,Jun Ma,Xiaodong Liu,Zhuo Li,Xiaoguang Mao
关键词: Multimodal Entity Linking, aligning the mention, Integrate Multimodal information, Dynamically Integrate Multimodal, study delves
中文关键词: 多模式实体链接、调整提及、集成多模式信息、动态集成多模式、研究深入研究
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: Published on PRCV24

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Our study delves into Multimodal Entity Linking, aligning the mention in multimodal information with entities in knowledge base. Existing methods are still facing challenges like ambiguous entity representations and limited image information utilization. Thus, we propose dynamic entity extraction using ChatGPT, which dynamically extracts entities and enhances datasets. We also propose a method: Dynamically Integrate Multimodal information with knowledge base (DIM), employing the capability of the Large Language Model (LLM) for visual understanding. The LLM, such as BLIP-2, extracts information relevant to entities in the image, which can facilitate improved extraction of entity features and linking them with the dynamic entity representations provided by ChatGPT. The experiments demonstrate that our proposed DIM method outperforms the majority of existing methods on the three original datasets, and achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) on the dynamically enhanced datasets (Wiki+, Rich+, Diverse+). For reproducibility, our code and collected datasets are released on \urlthis https URL.
摘要：我们的研究深入探讨了多通道实体链接，将多通道信息中提及的内容与知识库中的实体进行了对齐。现有的方法仍然面临着实体表示不明确、图像信息利用率有限等挑战。因此，我们提出了基于ChatGPT的动态实体提取，它动态地提取实体并增强数据集。我们还提出了一种方法：利用大语言模型的视觉理解能力，将多通道信息与知识库动态集成。LLM，如BLIP-2，提取与图像中实体相关的信息，这有助于改进实体特征的提取，并将它们与ChatGPT提供的动态实体表示联系起来。实验表明，我们提出的DIM方法在三个原始数据集上的性能优于现有的大多数方法，并且在动态增强的数据集(Wiki+、Rich+、DISTIZATION+)上达到了最先进的(SOTA)。为了可重现性，我们的代码和收集的数据集在此HTTPS URL上发布。

[NLP-64] Empirical Evaluation of Public HateSpeech Datasets
[NLP-64] 公共仇恨言论数据集的实证评估

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12018
作者: Sadar Jaf,Basel Barakat
关键词: extensive communication benefits, communication benefits offered, ensure user safety, hate speech, social media platforms
中文关键词: 广泛的沟通好处、提供的沟通好处、确保用户安全、仇恨言论、社交媒体平台
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 18 pages, 12 tables, 1 algorithm pseudocode, 7 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite the extensive communication benefits offered by social media platforms, numerous challenges must be addressed to ensure user safety. One of the most significant risks faced by users on these platforms is targeted hate speech. Social media platforms are widely utilised for generating datasets employed in training and evaluating machine learning algorithms for hate speech detection. However, existing public datasets exhibit numerous limitations, hindering the effective training of these algorithms and leading to inaccurate hate speech classification. This study provides a comprehensive empirical evaluation of several public datasets commonly used in automated hate speech classification. Through rigorous analysis, we present compelling evidence highlighting the limitations of current hate speech datasets. Additionally, we conduct a range of statistical analyses to elucidate the strengths and weaknesses inherent in these datasets. This work aims to advance the development of more accurate and reliable machine learning models for hate speech detection by addressing the dataset limitations identified.
摘要：尽管社交媒体平台提供了广泛的交流优势，但为了确保用户安全，必须应对众多挑战。这些平台上的用户面临的最大风险之一是有针对性的仇恨言论。社交媒体平台被广泛用于生成用于训练和评估仇恨语音检测的机器学习算法的数据集。然而，现有的公开数据集表现出许多局限性，阻碍了这些算法的有效训练，并导致了不准确的仇恨语音分类。本研究对仇恨言论自动分类中常用的几个公共数据集进行了全面的实证评估。通过严格的分析，我们提出了令人信服的证据，突显了当前仇恨言论数据集的局限性。此外，我们还进行了一系列的统计分析，以阐明这些数据集中固有的优势和劣势。这项工作的目的是通过解决已确定的数据集限制，推动开发更准确和可靠的机器学习模型用于仇恨语音检测。

[NLP-65] Follow-Up Questions Improve Documents Generated by Large Language Models
[NLP-65] 后续问题改进大型语言模型生成的文档

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12017
作者: Bernadette J Tix
关键词: Large Language Models, Language Models generating, Models generating follow, Large Language, Language Models
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，语言模型生成，模型生成遵循，大型语言，语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study investigates the impact of Large Language Models generating follow up questions in response to user requests for short text documents. Users provided prompts requesting documents they would like the AI to produce. The AI then generated questions to clarify the user needs before generating the requested documents. Users answered the questions and then indicated their preference between a document generated using both the initial prompt and the questions and answers, and a document generated using only the initial prompt, and gave feedback about their experience with the question-answering process. The findings of this study show clear benefits to question-asking both in document preference and in the qualitative user experience.
摘要：本研究调查了大型语言模型生成后续问题以响应用户对短文本文档的请求的影响。用户提供提示，请求他们希望人工智能生成的文档。然后，人工智能在生成请求的文档之前生成问题以澄清用户需求。用户回答问题，然后表明他们对使用初始提示和问题和答案生成的文档和仅使用初始提示生成的文档的偏好，并提供有关他们在问答过程中的体验的反馈。这项研究的结果表明，提问在文档偏好和定性用户体验方面都有明显的好处。

[NLP-66] LLM-based Frameworks for API Argument Filling in Task-Oriented Conversational Systems
[NLP-66] 基于LLM的面向任务对话系统API参数填充框架

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12016
作者: Jisoo Mok,Mohammad Kachuee,Shuyang Dai,Shayan Ray,Tara Taghavi,Sungroh Yoon
关键词: Task-orientated conversational agents, leveraging external APIs, argument filling, Task-orientated conversational, API argument filling
中文关键词: 面向任务的对话代理，利用外部API，参数填充，面向任务的对话，API参数填充
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Task-orientated conversational agents interact with users and assist them via leveraging external APIs. A typical task-oriented conversational system can be broken down into three phases: external API selection, argument filling, and response generation. The focus of our work is the task of argument filling, which is in charge of accurately providing arguments required by the selected API. Upon comprehending the dialogue history and the pre-defined API schema, the argument filling task is expected to provide the external API with the necessary information to generate a desirable agent action. In this paper, we study the application of Large Language Models (LLMs) for the problem of API argument filling task. Our initial investigation reveals that LLMs require an additional grounding process to successfully perform argument filling, inspiring us to design training and prompting frameworks to ground their responses. Our experimental results demonstrate that when paired with proposed techniques, the argument filling performance of LLMs noticeably improves, paving a new way toward building an automated argument filling framework.
摘要：面向任务的会话代理通过利用外部API与用户交互并帮助他们。一个典型的面向任务的对话系统可以分为三个阶段：外部API选择、参数填充和响应生成。我们的工作重点是参数填充任务，它负责准确地提供所选API所需的参数。在理解对话历史和预定义的API模式之后，参数填充任务预计将向外部API提供必要的信息以生成所需的代理动作。本文研究了大型语言模型在API参数填充问题中的应用。我们的初步调查显示，LLMS需要一个额外的扎根过程才能成功地执行论点填充，这激励我们设计培训并促使框架将他们的回答接地。我们的实验结果表明，当与所提出的技术相结合时，LLMS的参数填充性能显著提高，为构建自动参数填充框架铺平了一条新的道路。

[NLP-67] he Great AI Witch Hunt: Reviewers Perception and (Mis)Conception of Generative AI in Research Writing
[NLP-67] 伟大的人工智能猎巫：评论家在研究写作中对生成人工智能的看法和（不）概念

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12015
作者: Hilda Hadan,Derrick Wang,Reza Hadi Mogavi,Joseph Tu,Leah Zhang-Kennedy,Lennart E. Nacke
关键词: growing fast, Generative, writing, AI-augmented writing, AI-augmented
中文关键词: 快速增长，生成，写作，人工智能增强写作，人工智能增强
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Generative AI (GenAI) use in research writing is growing fast. However, it is unclear how peer reviewers recognize or misjudge AI-augmented manuscripts. To investigate the impact of AI-augmented writing on peer reviews, we conducted a snippet-based online survey with 17 peer reviewers from top-tier HCI conferences. Our findings indicate that while AI-augmented writing improves readability, language diversity, and informativeness, it often lacks research details and reflective insights from authors. Reviewers consistently struggled to distinguish between human and AI-augmented writing but their judgements remained consistent. They noted the loss of a “human touch” and subjective expressions in AI-augmented writing. Based on our findings, we advocate for reviewer guidelines that promote impartial evaluations of submissions, regardless of any personal biases towards GenAI. The quality of the research itself should remain a priority in reviews, regardless of any preconceived notions about the tools used to create it. We emphasize that researchers must maintain their authorship and control over the writing process, even when using GenAI’s assistance.
摘要：生成性人工智能(GenAI)在科研写作中的应用正在迅速增长。然而，目前尚不清楚同行评审员如何识别或误判人工智能增强的手稿。为了调查人工智能增强写作对同行评议的影响，我们对来自顶级HCI会议的17名同行评审员进行了一项基于片段的在线调查。我们的发现表明，虽然人工智能增强的写作提高了可读性、语言多样性和信息量，但它往往缺乏研究细节和作者的反思见解。评论家们一直在努力区分人类写作和人工智能增强写作，但他们的判断仍然是一致的。他们指出，在人工智能增强的写作中，“人性化”和主观表达的丧失。基于我们的发现，我们倡导审查者指南，促进对提交的公正评估，而不考虑对GenAI的任何个人偏见。研究本身的质量应该仍然是审查的优先事项，无论关于创建它的工具是否有任何先入为主的观念。我们强调，即使在使用GenAI的帮助下，研究人员也必须保持他们的作者身份和对写作过程的控制。

[NLP-68] Dating ancient manuscripts using radiocarbon and AI-based writing style analysis
[NLP-68] 使用放射性碳和基于人工智能的写作风格分析确定古代手稿的年代

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12013
作者: Mladen Popović,Maruf A. Dhali,Lambert Schomaker,Johannes van der Plicht,Kaare Lund Rasmussen,Jacopo La Nasa,Ilaria Degano,Maria Perla Colombini,Eibert Tigchelaar
关键词: Dead Sea Scrolls, evolution of ideas, essential for reconstructing, reconstructing the evolution, Enoch
中文关键词: 死海古卷，思想的演变，对于重建、重建演变至关重要，伊诺
类目: Digital Libraries (cs.DL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 16 pages of main article, 103 pages of supplementary materials, currently under review after having been submitted 20 July 2023

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Determining the chronology of ancient handwritten manuscripts is essential for reconstructing the evolution of ideas. For the Dead Sea Scrolls, this is particularly important. However, there is an almost complete lack of date-bearing manuscripts evenly distributed across the timeline and written in similar scripts available for palaeographic comparison. Here, we present Enoch, a state-of-the-art AI-based date-prediction model, trained on the basis of new radiocarbon-dated samples of the scrolls. Enoch uses established handwriting-style descriptors and applies Bayesian ridge regression. The challenge of this study is that the number of radiocarbon-dated manuscripts is small, while current machine learning requires an abundance of training data. We show that by using combined angular and allographic writing style feature vectors and applying Bayesian ridge regression, Enoch could predict the radiocarbon-based dates from style, supported by leave-one-out validation, with varied MAEs of 27.9 to 30.7 years relative to the radiocarbon dating. Enoch was then used to estimate the dates of 135 unseen manuscripts, revealing that 79 per cent of the samples were considered ‘realistic’ upon palaeographic post-hoc evaluation. We present a new chronology of the scrolls. The radiocarbon ranges and Enoch’s style-based predictions are often older than the traditionally assumed palaeographic estimates. In the range of 300-50 BCE, Enoch’s date prediction provides an improved granularity. The study is in line with current developments in multimodal machine-learning techniques, and the methods can be used for date prediction in other partially-dated manuscript collections. This research shows how Enoch’s quantitative, probability-based approach can be a tool for palaeographers and historians, re-dating ancient Jewish key texts and contributing to current debates on Jewish and Christian origins.
摘要：确定古代手写手稿的年代是重建思想演变的关键。对于《死海古卷》来说，这一点尤为重要。然而，几乎完全缺乏在时间线上均匀分布的日期手稿，并以类似的文字写成，用于古地理比较。在这里，我们介绍了Enoch，一个最先进的基于人工智能的日期预测模型，基于新的放射性碳测年卷轴样本进行训练。Enoch使用已建立的手写风格描述符，并应用贝叶斯岭回归。这项研究的挑战是，放射性碳年代确定的手稿数量很少，而目前的机器学习需要大量的训练数据。结果表明，通过组合角度和异形体书写风格特征向量，并应用贝叶斯岭回归，Enoch可以根据风格预测放射性碳基年代，并得到留一检验的支持，相对于放射性碳测年，MAE变化27.9~30.7年。然后，Enoch被用来估计135份未见过的手稿的日期，结果显示，79%的样本在古地理后评估中被认为是“真实的”。我们提出了一种新的古卷年表。放射性碳范围和伊诺克基于风格的预测往往比传统假设的古地理估计更古老。在公元前300-50年的范围内，Enoch的日期预测提供了更好的粒度。这项研究与目前多模式机器学习技术的发展是一致的，这些方法可以用于其他部分日期手稿收藏的日期预测。这项研究表明，伊诺克的量化、基于概率的方法如何成为古文学家和历史学家的工具，重新确定古代犹太关键文献的年代，并为当前关于犹太和基督教起源的辩论做出贡献。

[NLP-69] Specific language impairment (SLI) detection pipeline from transcriptions of spontaneous narratives
[NLP-69] 来自自发叙述转录的特定语言障碍（SLI）检测管道

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12012
作者: Santiago Arena,Antonio Quintero-Rincón
关键词: Specific Language Impairment, affects communication, Specific Language, Language Impairment, comprehension and expression
中文关键词: 特定语言障碍，影响沟通，特定语言，语言障碍，理解和表达
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Applications (stat.AP); Machine Learning (stat.ML)
备注: 15 pages, in Spanish language, 4 figures, 3 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Specific Language Impairment (SLI) is a disorder that affects communication and can affect both comprehension and expression. This study focuses on effectively detecting SLI in children using transcripts of spontaneous narratives from 1063 interviews. A three-stage cascading pipeline was proposed f. In the first stage, feature extraction and dimensionality reduction of the data are performed using the Random Forest (RF) and Spearman correlation methods. In the second stage, the most predictive variables from the first stage are estimated using logistic regression, which is used in the last stage to detect SLI in children from transcripts of spontaneous narratives using a nearest neighbor classifier. The results revealed an accuracy of 97.13% in identifying SLI, highlighting aspects such as the length of the responses, the quality of their utterances, and the complexity of the language. This new approach, framed in natural language processing, offers significant benefits to the field of SLI detection by avoiding complex subjective variables and focusing on quantitative metrics directly related to the child’s performance.
摘要：特异性语言障碍(SLI)是一种影响交流的障碍，可影响理解和表达。本研究旨在利用1063次访谈中自发叙述的文本，有效地检测儿童的SLI。提出了一种三级级联流水线。在第一级，利用随机森林(RF)和Spearman相关方法对数据进行特征提取和降维。在第二阶段，使用Logistic回归估计第一阶段中最具预测性的变量，并在最后阶段使用最近邻分类器从自发叙述的转录中检测儿童的SLI。结果显示，在识别SLI方面的准确率为97.13%，突出了回答的长度、话语的质量和语言的复杂性等方面。这种以自然语言处理为框架的新方法，通过避免复杂的主观变量并关注与儿童表现直接相关的量化指标，为SLI检测领域提供了显著的好处。

[NLP-70] People will agree what I think: Investigating LLMs False Consensus Effect
[NLP-70] 人们会同意我的想法：调查LLM虚假共识效应

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12007
作者: Junhyuk Choi,Yeseon Hong,Bugeun Kim
关键词: Large Language Models, Large Language, Language Models, interactive systems requiring, False Consensus Effect
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、大型语言、语言模型、交互式系统需要、虚假共识效应
类目: Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Under review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently been widely adopted on interactive systems requiring communications. As the false belief in a model can harm the usability of such systems, LLMs should not have cognitive biases that humans have. Especially psychologists focused on the False Consensus Effect (FCE), which can distract smooth communication by posing false beliefs. However, previous studies have less examined FCE in LLMs thoroughly, which needs more consideration of confounding biases, general situations, and prompt changes. Therefore, in this paper, we conduct two studies to deeply examine the FCE phenomenon in LLMs. In Study 1, we investigate whether LLMs have FCE. In Study 2, we explore how various prompting styles affect the demonstration of FCE. As a result of these studies, we identified that popular LLMs have FCE. Also, the result specifies the conditions when the strength of FCE becomes larger or smaller compared to normal usage.
摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）最近被广泛采用在需要通信的交互式系统上。由于对模型的错误信念可能会损害此类系统的可用性，LLM不应该有人类所具有的认知偏差。尤其是心理学家关注虚假共识效应（FCE），它可能会通过提出虚假信念来分散流畅的沟通。然而，之前的研究很少彻底审查法学硕士的FCE，这需要更多地考虑混淆偏见、一般情况和迅速的变化。因此，在本文中，我们进行了两项研究来深入探讨LLM中的FCE现象。在研究1中，我们调查LLM是否具有FCE。在研究2中，我们探索了各种提示风格如何影响FCE的表现。作为这些研究的结果，我们发现流行的LLM具有FCE。此外，结果还指定了FCE强度与正常使用相比变得更大或更小的条件。

[NLP-71] he Kolmogorov Complexity of Irish traditional dance music
[NLP-71] 他Kolmogorov爱尔兰传统舞曲的复杂性

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12000
作者: Michael McGettrick,Paul McGettrick
关键词: Irish traditional dance, traditional dance music, estimate the Kolmogorov, melodies in Irish, Irish traditional
中文关键词: 爱尔兰传统舞蹈，传统舞曲，估计Kolmogorov，爱尔兰旋律，爱尔兰传统
类目: Information Theory (cs.IT); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: 6 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We estimate the Kolmogorov complexity of melodies in Irish traditional dance music using Lempel-Ziv compression. The “tunes” of the music are presented in so-called “ABC notation” as simply a sequence of letters from an alphabet: We have no rhythmic variation, with all notes being of equal length. Our estimation of algorithmic complexity can be used to distinguish “simple” or “easy” tunes (with more repetition) from “difficult” ones (with less repetition) which should prove useful for students learning tunes. We further present a comparison of two tune categories (reels and jigs) in terms of their complexity.
摘要：我们使用Lempel-Ziv压缩来估计爱尔兰传统舞曲中旋律的Kolmogorov复杂性。音乐的“曲调”以所谓的“ABC记法”形式呈现，简单地表示为字母表中的字母序列：我们没有节奏变化，所有音符的长度相等。我们对算法复杂性的估计可以用于区分“简单”或“简单”曲调（重复较多）与“困难”曲调（重复较少），这应该对学生学习曲调有用。我们进一步比较了两种曲调类别（卷轴和吉格）的复杂性。

[NLP-72] Generating Harder Cross-document Event Coreference Resolution Datasets using Metaphoric Paraphrasing
[NLP-72] 使用隐喻解释生成更难的跨文档事件共指解析数据集

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11988
作者: Shafiuddin Rehan Ahmed,Zhiyong Eric Wang,George Arthur Baker,Kevin Stowe,James H. Martin
关键词: Event Coreference Resolution, popular Cross-Document Event, Cross-Document Event Coreference, Coreference Resolution, coreferring event triggers
中文关键词: 事件共指涉解决、流行的跨文档事件、跨文档事件共指涉、共指解决、共指事件触发器
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Short Paper, ACL 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The most popular Cross-Document Event Coreference Resolution (CDEC) datasets fail to convey the true difficulty of the task, due to the lack of lexical diversity between coreferring event triggers (words or phrases that refer to an event). Furthermore, there is a dearth of event datasets for figurative language, limiting a crucial avenue of research in event comprehension. We address these two issues by introducing ECB+META, a lexically rich variant of Event Coref Bank Plus (ECB+) for CDEC on symbolic and metaphoric language. We use ChatGPT as a tool for the metaphoric transformation of sentences in the documents of ECB+, then tag the original event triggers in the transformed sentences in a semi-automated manner. In this way, we avoid the re-annotation of expensive coreference links. We present results that show existing methods that work well on ECB+ struggle with ECB+META, thereby paving the way for CDEC research on a much more challenging dataset. Code/data: this https URL
摘要：由于共指事件触发器（指事件的单词或短语）之间缺乏词汇多样性，最流行的跨文档事件共指解析（CDEC）数据集无法传达任务的真正难度。此外，缺乏比喻语言的事件数据集，限制了事件理解研究的重要途径。我们通过引入ECB+META来解决这两个问题，ECB +META是CDEC的Events Coref Bank Plus（ECB+）的词汇丰富变体，涉及符号和隐喻语言。我们使用ChatGPT作为ECB+文档中句子的隐喻转换工具，然后以半自动化的方式在转换后的句子中标记原始事件触发器。这样，我们就避免了对昂贵的共同引用链接的重新注释。我们提供的结果表明，现有方法在ECB+与ECB+Meta的斗争中效果良好，从而为CDEC对更具挑战性的数据集的研究铺平了道路。代码/数据：此https URL

[NLP-73] Mimetic Poet
[NLP-73] 模仿诗人

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11984
作者: Jon McCormack,Elliott Wilson,Nina Rajcic,Maria Teresa Llano
关键词: facilitate creative ideation, paper presents, initial assessment, device, presents the design
中文关键词: 促进创造性构思、论文呈现、初步评估、设备、呈现设计
类目: Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Paper accepted at the International Conference on Computational Creativity, (ICCC 2024), Jönköping, Sweden

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This paper presents the design and initial assessment of a novel device that uses generative AI to facilitate creative ideation, inspiration, and reflective thought. Inspired by magnetic poetry, which was originally designed to help overcome writer’s block, the device allows participants to compose short poetic texts from a limited vocabulary by physically placing words on the device’s surface. Upon composing the text, the system employs a large language model (LLM) to generate a response, displayed on an e-ink screen. We explored various strategies for internally sequencing prompts to foster creative thinking, including analogy, allegorical interpretations, and ideation. We installed the device in our research laboratory for two weeks and held a focus group at the conclusion to evaluate the design. The design choice to limit interactions with the LLM to poetic text, coupled with the tactile experience of assembling the poem, fostered a deeper and more enjoyable engagement with the LLM compared to traditional chatbot or screen-based interactions. This approach gives users the opportunity to reflect on the AI-generated responses in a manner conducive to creative thought.
摘要：本文介绍了一种新型设备的设计和初步评估，该设备使用生成性人工智能来促进创造性思维、灵感和反思性思维。受磁性诗歌的启发，该设备最初旨在帮助克服作家的障碍，允许参与者通过将单词物理地放置在设备表面来用有限的词汇撰写简短的诗歌文本。在编写文本时，系统使用大型语言模型(LLM)来生成响应，并显示在电子墨水屏幕上。我们探索了各种内部排序提示的策略，以培养创造性思维，包括类比、寓言解释和思维。我们在我们的研究实验室安装了两周的设备，并在结束时召开了一个焦点小组来评估设计。与传统的聊天机器人或基于屏幕的互动相比，将与LLM的互动限制在诗意文本的设计选择，再加上组装诗歌的触觉体验，培养了与LLM更深层次和更愉快的互动。这种方法让用户有机会以一种有利于创造性思维的方式反思人工智能产生的反应。

[NLP-74] Open the Data! Chuvash Datasets
[NLP-74] 打开数据！楚瓦什数据集

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11982
作者: Nikolay Plotnikov,Alexander Antonov
关键词: enhance linguistic research, Chuvash language, aiming to support, introduce four comprehensive, support and enhance
中文关键词: 加强语言学研究，楚瓦什语言，旨在支持，引入四个全面，支持和增强
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this paper, we introduce four comprehensive datasets for the Chuvash language, aiming to support and enhance linguistic research and technological development for this underrepresented language. These datasets include a monolingual dataset, a parallel dataset with Russian, a parallel dataset with English, and an audio dataset. Each dataset is meticulously curated to serve various applications such as machine translation, linguistic analysis, and speech recognition, providing valuable resources for scholars and developers working with the Chuvash language. Together, these datasets represent a significant step towards preserving and promoting the Chuvash language in the digital age.
摘要：在本文中，我们介绍了Chuvash语言的四个全面数据集，旨在支持和加强这种代表性不足的语言的语言研究和技术开发。这些数据集包括单语数据集、俄语并行数据集、英语并行数据集和音频数据集。每个数据集都经过精心策划，以服务于机器翻译、语言分析和语音识别等各种应用，为使用Chuvash语言的学者和开发人员提供宝贵的资源。这些数据集共同代表着在数字时代保护和推广楚瓦什语言的重要一步。

[NLP-75] SDS – Text-to-Speech Distribution Score
[NLP-75] SDs --文本到语音分布分数

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12707
作者: Christoph Minixhofer,Ondřej Klejch,Peter Bell
关键词: produce audio close, systems produce audio, recently published, produce audio, audio close
中文关键词: 产生音频关闭，系统产生音频，最近发布，产生音频，音频关闭
类目: Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Sound (cs.SD)
备注: Under review for SLT 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Many recently published Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems produce audio close to real speech. However, TTS evaluation needs to be revisited to make sense of the results obtained with the new architectures, approaches and datasets. We propose evaluating the quality of synthetic speech as a combination of multiple factors such as prosody, speaker identity, and intelligibility. Our approach assesses how well synthetic speech mirrors real speech by obtaining correlates of each factor and measuring their distance from both real speech datasets and noise datasets. We benchmark 35 TTS systems developed between 2008 and 2024 and show that our score computed as an unweighted average of factors strongly correlates with the human evaluations from each time period.
摘要：许多最近发布的文本转语音（TTC）系统产生接近真实语音的音频。然而，需要重新审视TTC评估，以理解使用新架构、方法和数据集获得的结果。我们建议将合成语音的质量评估为韵律、说话者身份和清晰度等多种因素的组合。我们的方法通过获取每个因素的相关性并测量它们与真实语音数据集和噪音数据集的距离来评估合成语音反映真实语音的程度。我们对2008年至2024年期间开发的35个TTC系统进行了基准测试，并表明我们的分数作为因素的未加权平均值计算出来，与每个时间段的人类评估密切相关。

[NLP-76] LiteGPT: Large Vision-Language Model for Joint Chest X-ray Localization and Classification Task
[NLP-76] LiteGPT：用于关节胸部X射线定位和分类任务的大型视觉语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12064
作者: Khai Le-Duc,Ryan Zhang,Ngoc Son Nguyen,Tan-Hanh Pham,Anh Dao,Ba Hung Ngo,Anh Totti Nguyen,Truong-Son Hy
关键词: imaging remains underexplored, achieving satisfactory performance, medical imaging remains, achieving satisfactory, remains underexplored
中文关键词: 成像仍然未充分探索，实现令人满意的性能，医学成像仍然未充分探索
类目: Image and Video Processing (eess.IV); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Multimedia (cs.MM)
备注: Preprint, 19 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Vision-language models have been extensively explored across a wide range of tasks, achieving satisfactory performance; however, their application in medical imaging remains underexplored. In this work, we propose a unified framework - LiteGPT - for the medical imaging. We leverage multiple pre-trained visual encoders to enrich information and enhance the performance of vision-language models. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study to utilize vision-language models for the novel task of joint localization and classification in medical images. Besides, we are pioneers in providing baselines for disease localization in chest X-rays. Finally, we set new state-of-the-art performance in the image classification task on the well-benchmarked VinDr-CXR dataset. All code and models are publicly available online: this https URL
摘要：视觉语言模型在广泛的任务中得到了广泛的探索，取得了令人满意的性能;然而，它们在医学成像中的应用仍然没有得到充分的探索。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个用于医学成像的统一框架- LiteGPT。我们利用多个预先训练的视觉编码器来丰富信息并增强视觉语言模型的性能。据我们所知，这是第一项利用视觉语言模型来完成医学图像中联合定位和分类的新任务的研究。此外，我们是为胸部X光检查疾病定位提供基线的先驱。最后，我们在经过完善基准的VinDr-CXR数据集上的图像分类任务中设置了新的最先进性能。所有代码和型号均在线公开：此https URL

计算机视觉

[CV-0] SMooDi: Stylized Motion Diffusion Model

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12783
作者: Lei Zhong,Yiming Xie,Varun Jampani,Deqing Sun,Huaizu Jiang
关键词: Stylized Motion Diffusion, Motion Diffusion model, Diffusion model, Motion Diffusion, stylized motion driven
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Graphics (cs.GR)
*备注: ECCV 2024. Project page: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We introduce a novel Stylized Motion Diffusion model, dubbed SMooDi, to generate stylized motion driven by content texts and style motion sequences. Unlike existing methods that either generate motion of various content or transfer style from one sequence to another, SMooDi can rapidly generate motion across a broad range of content and diverse styles. To this end, we tailor a pre-trained text-to-motion model for stylization. Specifically, we propose style guidance to ensure that the generated motion closely matches the reference style, alongside a lightweight style adaptor that directs the motion towards the desired style while ensuring realism. Experiments across various applications demonstrate that our proposed framework outperforms existing methods in stylized motion generation.

[CV-1] Contrastive Adversarial Training for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.12782
作者: Jiahong Chen,Zhilin Zhang,Lucy Li,Behzad Shahrasbi,Arjun Mishra
关键词: finding domain invariant, domain adaptation tasks, invariant feature representations, Domain, domain invariant feature
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
*备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Domain adversarial training has shown its effective capability for finding domain invariant feature representations and been successfully adopted for various domain adaptation tasks. However, recent advances of large models (e.g., vision transformers) and emerging of complex adaptation scenarios (e.g., DomainNet) make adversarial training being easily biased towards source domain and hardly adapted to target domain. The reason is twofold: relying on large amount of labelled data from source domain for large model training and lacking of labelled data from target domain for fine-tuning. Existing approaches widely focused on either enhancing discriminator or improving the training stability for the backbone networks. Due to unbalanced competition between the feature extractor and the discriminator during the adversarial training, existing solutions fail to function well on complex datasets. To address this issue, we proposed a novel contrastive adversarial training (CAT) approach that leverages the labeled source domain samples to reinforce and regulate the feature generation for target domain. Typically, the regulation forces the target feature distribution being similar to the source feature distribution.
